channel.title: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism.
channel.description: KillBait is a news aggregator developed by students and professionals in journalism and web development from Argentina, Spain, and the United Kingdom. Our focus is on providing readers with clear, concise, and relevant summaries of each news story.

item.title: What is KillBait?
item.description: Surely you've clicked on a news article with an eye-catching or sensational headline...

item.title: How KillBait works
item.description: Our editors, along with any registered user, can submit news they find interesting simply by providing the URL to the system.

item.title: Frecuently Asked Questions
item.description: Common Questions about KillBait

item.title: Top rated media
item.description: Best media: This classification is based on the votes cast by the users of this website on each news item from different media, and it does not necessarily reflect reality.

item.title: Lowest rated media
item.description: Worst media: This classification is based on the votes cast by the users of this website on each news item from different media, and it does not necessarily reflect reality.

item.title: Upcoming news
Iranian Hacker Group Handala Linked to Retaliatory Cyberattacks on US and Israeli Targets
item.description: Since late February, following a series of air strikes by the United States and Israel on Iran, cybersecurity experts have warned of potential Iranian retaliatory attacks. These warnings became a reality with the breach of Michigan-based medical technology firm Stryker, which reportedly disabled tens of thousands of computers globally. The attack has been claimed by an Iranian hacker group called Handala, which experts believe operates as a front for Iran’s Ministry of Intelligence (MOIS). Handala combines the public-facing persona of a hacktivist group with destructive state-sponsored cyber capabilities, targeting Israeli businesses, government agencies, and now US companies with both wiper malware and hack-and-leak operations. Previously associated with pro-Palestinian rhetoric, the group has escalated its activity amid the ongoing conflict, leveraging opportunities to disrupt Western organizations. Analysts suggest that while Handala’s operations are highly disruptive, they often la

item.title: Reflections on Human and Artificial Intelligence
item.description: The article explores the nature of human intelligence in comparison with artificial intelligence (AI). The author argues that AI is often described as merely simulating reasoning, functioning through statistical predictions of word sequences rather than true thought. Surprisingly, the author suggests that human cognition might operate in a similar way. For example, a journalist writing an article intuitively knows word patterns and structures without consciously understanding grammatical rules, resembling an AI's pattern-based predictions. The article further discusses how AI, despite its known limitations and occasional errors, can sometimes produce reasoning that surpasses typical human performance. While humans are constrained by their cultural and experiential learning, AI can process vast amounts of information and generate structured arguments, although still dependent on its training data. The author concludes with the observation that AI technology is in its infancy and holds s

item.title: Congress Proposes Legislation to Require Warrants for FBI Access to Americans’ Communications
item.description: A bipartisan group of U.S. lawmakers has introduced the Government Surveillance Reform Act of 2026, aiming to impose strict warrant requirements on the FBI’s access to Americans’ communications collected under Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act. The legislation would close the so-called 'backdoor search' loophole that allows the FBI to read private messages of Americans without a warrant and prohibit reverse targeting, where foreign surveillance is used to monitor domestic individuals. The bill also bans the federal purchase of commercial data on U.S. residents, including location and browsing information, and restricts law enforcement from exploiting outdated statutory authorities. Senators Ron Wyden and Mike Lee, alongside Representatives Warren Davidson and Zoe Lofgren, lead the effort, citing recent court rulings that found the FBI’s warrantless searches unconstitutional. The legislation comes amid dismantled internal oversight mechanisms within the FBI and th

item.title: Trump Criticizes Obama and Biden on Stair Climbing During Rally
item.description: During a rally in Hebron, Kentucky, President Donald Trump drew attention to what many considered a trivial complaint about former President Barack Obama’s way of descending stairs. Trump remarked to the audience that the only thing he 'admired' about Obama was his stair technique, describing it as 'unpresidential' yet somehow successful without a major fall. He contrasted this with President Joe Biden, claiming that Biden struggled and fell multiple times while ascending stairs. This is not the first time Trump has publicly commented on the physical movements of his predecessors; he previously criticized Obama’s stair descent on a podcast in February, calling it inelegant and risky. Trump also praised his own cautious walking style during a military gathering, emphasizing the importance of safety over speed or records. Observers note that while Trump’s comments are unusual and largely focus on trivial matters, they reflect his tendency to compare himself to previous presidents in high

item.title: Ongoing Iran Conflict Escalates: Strait of Hormuz Closed, Oil Prices Surge, and Regional Tensions Rise
item.description: The conflict between Iran, the United States, and Israel has entered its 13th day, with Iran’s new Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, declaring that the strategic Strait of Hormuz will remain closed as a leverage tool. Attacks on oil tankers and energy infrastructure have caused historic disruptions in the global oil market, with Brent crude hovering around $100 per barrel. Iran’s electricity and military infrastructure are under threat, and the country warns of severe consequences if targeted. Israel is preparing for expanded operations against Hezbollah in Lebanon, with evacuation orders affecting hundreds of thousands of residents and the potential for one million displaced within days. Environmental groups like Greenpeace warn of ecological risks due to stranded oil tankers, highlighting the potential for catastrophic oil spills in the Persian Gulf. Meanwhile, US officials acknowledge the Navy’s current inability to escort vessels safely through the strait and are reinforc

item.title: Iranian Attacks on Middle East Energy Infrastructure Push Oil Prices Back Above $100
item.description: Oil prices have surged past the $100 per barrel mark due to attacks on Middle East energy infrastructure, mainly attributed to Iran. Brent crude briefly reached $101 after Iranian strikes targeted two fuel tankers in Iraqi waters near Basra, using explosive-laden boats. Iraq responded by halting operations at all its oil ports, and further incidents have been reported in Dubai and Bahrain. Analysts warn that these targeted attacks could cause long-term damage to oil output, prolonging the disruption even after the conflict ends. Although prices dropped from a recent peak of $118 after the International Energy Agency announced a coordinated release of strategic reserves from over 30 nations, attacks continued to intensify, limiting the impact on market prices. The IEA estimates that global oil supply has been reduced by around eight million barrels per day, equating to roughly 10% of global production. This disruption is already affecting UK energy costs, with diesel prices up 9% since

item.title: Preliminary report suggests outdated US intelligence likely caused deadly strike on Iranian school
item.description: A preliminary investigation has indicated that the United States military was 'likely responsible' for a missile strike on Shajareh Tayyebeh Elementary School in Minab, southern Iran, which killed more than 170 people, most of them children. The strike occurred on February 28, the first day of a near two-week conflict targeting Iranian military facilities. Reports suggest that outdated US intelligence identified the school as an Iranian Revolutionary Guards Corps (IRGC) base. Satellite analysis indicates that the site was clearly a civilian school, with bright murals and accessible online information about students and staff. Evidence also shows a US-made Tomahawk missile hit the compound near the school. US President Donald Trump initially blamed Iran or other countries and later deferred to investigative findings. Some US officials, including members of Congress, criticized failures in civilian protection procedures, including the dismantling of the Pentagon's Civilian Protection Cen

item.title: Guide to Streaming and Watching the 98th Academy Awards
item.description: The 98th Annual Academy Awards will take place on Sunday, March 15, 2026, and fans have multiple ways to watch the ceremony. The Oscars will stream live on Hulu, accessible on all subscription tiers, and can also be viewed via local ABC stations on cable or over the air. For those who subscribe to streaming TV services that carry ABC, such as YouTube TV, DirecTV, or Fubo, the broadcast is available as well. International viewers can access the show through ABC's global partners, though geo-blocking may require the use of a VPN in some countries. The red carpet preshow begins 30 minutes before the main event, giving viewers a chance to see fashion highlights and celebrity arrivals. Conan O'Brien will host the ceremony again, after promoting the role on several media platforms. For those interested in live commentary, communities such as Reddit's r/Oscars and various social media feeds on Bluesky offer interactive discussion during the broadcast. Overall, this year’s Oscars feature a div

item.title: Republican allies push Trump to maintain aggressive migrant deportation policies despite internal concerns
item.description:
The article describes growing tensions inside President Donald Trump’s political and governing coalition over how aggressively his administration should pursue migrant deportations. A group of hardline allies and advocacy organizations is pressuring the White House to maintain or even expand a broad, punitive deportation agenda, rather than narrowing its focus to violent offenders. These allies reacted angrily after senior officials appeared to soften their rhetoric, emphasizing the removal of violent criminals instead of mass deportations.
The piece explains that the administration has recently shifted its public messaging and made notable leadership changes at the Department of Homeland Security (DHS). Border czar Tom Homan replaced CBP chief Greg Bovino in Minneapolis and significantly reduced the local enforcement footprint, and Trump fired DHS Secretary Kristi Noem, naming Senator Markwayne Mullin as her replacement. In his State of the Union address, Trump largely highlighted bo

item.title: Roger Reutimann's YAWN: A Bauhaus-Inspired Concrete Nightlight with Subtle Personality
item.description: Swiss designer Roger Reutimann has created YAWN, a unique hand-cast concrete nightlight that combines industrial minimalism with a touch of whimsy. Crafted entirely in his studio using custom molds, vibration techniques to remove air pockets, and meticulous sanding and sealing processes, the piece achieves precise, clean edges and a solid, architectural presence. The lamp's form draws heavily from Bauhaus principles, featuring strict geometry with stacked blocks, sharp right angles, and a cantilevered vertical element that conveys clarity, structure, and material honesty. Despite concrete's typical association with heavy, utilitarian construction, YAWN feels surprisingly alive thanks to its two recessed, softly glowing 'eyes'—diffused resin lenses powered by dimmable, smart-home compatible LEDs embedded within the concrete without disrupting its structural integrity or silhouette. Positioned beneath a thick, brow-like overhang, these glowing features create an expression of tiredness o

item.title: Man Arrested for Posing as Rideshare Driver and Assaulting Woman in Orange County
item.description: Authorities in Costa Mesa, California, arrested 43-year-old Felipe Rico-Ceballos after he allegedly lured an intoxicated woman into his vehicle under the pretense of giving her a ride home. The incident occurred on February 27 in Newport Beach, where the victim was later taken to a hospital. During the investigation, police determined that Rico-Ceballos was not operating as a legitimate rideshare driver at the time of the assault. According to police reports, he drove the woman to a different location, sexually assaulted her, and then abandoned her at a third location. On March 5, law enforcement officials arrested Rico-Ceballos on charges of sexual assault and kidnapping. Authorities are now seeking to identify any additional victims who may have encountered him. The Costa Mesa Police Department is urging anyone with information to contact Detective Peralta or Sergeant Jacobi. This case highlights the dangers of individuals misrepresenting themselves as rideshare drivers and raises co

item.title: Lobbyists Push to Shield Oil Companies from Climate Change Lawsuits
item.description: The article highlights ongoing efforts by the fossil fuel industry and its political allies to prevent legal accountability for climate-related damages. Across the U.S., including California, state and local governments are pursuing lawsuits against major oil companies like Exxon Mobil, Chevron, Shell, and BP, accusing them of deliberately misleading the public about the dangers of climate change. Taxpayers currently bear over $1 trillion annually in costs from climate disasters such as fires, floods, droughts, and storms. In response, Big Oil has lobbied Congress and leveraged the Supreme Court to seek immunity from lawsuits, echoing tactics used by gun, tobacco, and opioid industries. Organizations like the American Petroleum Institute prioritize shielding oil companies from what they term 'abusive' litigation, while proposed legislation aims to block communities from pursuing claims in court. The article argues that juries are a cornerstone of democracy and that allowing corporation

item.title: Archaeologists Discover Oldest Mithras Temple in Bavaria
item.description: Archaeologists in Regensburg, Germany, have uncovered a rare Roman temple linked to the secretive Mithras cult, marking the oldest known sanctuary of its kind in Bavaria. The temple, built during the first or second century A.D., was dedicated to Mithras, a deity associated with light and cosmic order. Mithraism, a mystery cult that coexisted with early Christianity, involved rituals that only select individuals, known as mystae, could participate in, with some attaining up to seven levels of initiation. The discovery came during preparations for a construction project and was initially overlooked until post-excavation analysis revealed distinctive tableware, ritual deposits, and a silver votive plaque inscribed 'DEO INVICTO,' confirming the site as a Mithraeum. Evidence also included animal bones used in sacrifices and various ceremonial objects. The temple reportedly burned down in 171 A.D. during the Marcomannic Wars and was not rebuilt, although some cult items may have been reused

item.title: René Redzepi Resigns from Noma Amid Allegations of Past Physical and Psychological Abuse
item.description: René Redzepi, the founder and head chef of the renowned three-Michelin-star restaurant Noma in Copenhagen, has resigned following renewed allegations of workplace bullying and abuse. The claims, detailed in a New York Times investigation, include accounts from dozens of former employees accusing him of physical violence—such as hitting, jabbing, shoving, and punching staff—and psychological mistreatment over many years, particularly during high-pressure periods. These historic issues resurfaced just as Noma prepared to launch a high-profile pop-up residency in Los Angeles' Silver Lake neighborhood, where tickets sold out quickly at $1,500 per package. Major sponsors like American Express and Blackbird withdrew support amid the controversy, and protesters, including former fermentation director Jason Ignacio White, gathered outside the venue. In an Instagram post, Redzepi acknowledged his past harmful behavior, stating he has worked to improve his leadership and that Noma has implemente

item.title: The Evolution and Enduring Legacy of the Polaroid Camera
item.description: The Polaroid camera, a symbol of instant photography, was born from a simple question asked by Edwin Land's three-year-old daughter in 1943: why couldn't she see her photo immediately? This curiosity sparked Land’s mental breakthrough, leading to the invention of instant photography. The first Polaroid Land Camera went on sale in 1948 and sold out immediately, offering sepia-toned prints in about a minute. Over the next decades, Polaroid cameras evolved, with the 1972 SX-70 model becoming an iconic piece of design, combining portability, elegance, and technological innovation. Polaroid dominated the instant photography market for decades, becoming a cultural fixture in art, journalism, and everyday life. However, the rise of digital photography disrupted the industry, and Polaroid went bankrupt in 2001, ceasing film production by 2007. Enthusiasts rescued the brand through The Impossible Project, reviving instant film with new, imperfect batches that captured the medium’s charm. By 202

item.title: Study Reveals Cellular Energy Imbalance in Young Adults with Major Depressive Disorder
item.description:
Researchers from the University of Queensland, in collaboration with the University of Minnesota, have identified a novel cellular mechanism potentially underlying major depressive disorder (MDD) in young adults. The study examined adenosine triphosphate (ATP), the primary energy molecule in cells, in both brain tissue via advanced imaging and blood cells from participants aged 18 to 25 diagnosed with MDD, compared to healthy controls.
Surprisingly, individuals with depression showed higher ATP production rates in the brain's visual cortex at rest and elevated ATP levels in peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMCs). However, when cells were subjected to stress or mitochondrial uncoupling, those from the depression group exhibited a reduced capacity to ramp up ATP production. This pattern suggests an early compensatory overactivity in energy generation that may exhaust cellular resources over time, contributing to hallmark symptoms like persistent fatigue, low motivation, reduced cogn

item.title: New Study Explains How Cats Use Flexible Spines to Land on Their Feet
item.description: A recent study published in The Anatomical Record sheds new light on the long-standing 'falling cat problem,' explaining why cats can almost always land on their feet. Led by physiologist Yasuo Higurashi at Yamaguchi University, the research shows that cats possess a highly flexible section in their upper thoracic spine, allowing them to twist their upper bodies independently from their lower bodies while in midair. This flexibility enables cats to first rotate their front legs and torso to face the ground, then adjust their hind legs accordingly, supporting the 'legs in, legs out' model of feline aerial maneuvering. The study also found evidence of a right-side turning bias in cats, with some animals consistently turning right during falls. These findings help clarify the biomechanics behind a cat's acrobatic ability, highlighting that their unique spinal structure is central to this skill. While more research is needed to fully confirm which of the existing models is correct, the res

item.title: SILICE: Spain's Pioneering Open Scientific Information System Based on Non-Commercial Sources
item.description: The article critiques the commercialization of scientific information systems, highlighting how private publishers and databases like Web of Science, Scopus, and Dimensions profit from research produced by publicly funded scientists who receive no royalties. This model transfers public money to private companies and compromises scientific independence by prioritizing commercial interests over objective evaluation. The author explains that traditional metrics, such as journal impact factors and simple citation counts, have distorted research assessment, encouraging quantity over quality and contributing to issues like paper mills, citation farms, and predatory practices. Initiatives like the Declaration on Research Assessment (DORA), the Leiden Manifesto, CoARA, the Barcelona Declaration on Open Research Information, and Spain's National Strategy for Open Science call for shifting to non-commercial, open infrastructures that rely on sources like Crossref, PubMed, OpenCitations, and Open

item.title: Russia Claims to Intercept Two British Long-Range Cruise Missiles Supplied to Ukraine
item.description: On March 12, 2026, Russian Defense Minister Andrei Belusov confirmed that two Storm Shadow long-range cruise missiles supplied by the United Kingdom to Ukraine were intercepted and destroyed between March 10 and 11. The announcement followed a Ukrainian strike targeting an industrial facility in Russia's Bryansk region, which Ukrainian intelligence identified as a key site for missile component production. The attack resulted in six fatalities and 42 injuries, prompting a rapid response from emergency services. Bryansk, approximately 100 kilometers from the Ukrainian border, has increasingly been the focus of aerial attacks attributed to Ukrainian forces, while Ukraine claims the region plays a significant role in Russian military production. Kremlin spokesperson Dmitri Peskov suggested that the missile strikes could not have occurred without direct involvement from UK military specialists. The report also highlights the intelligence cooperation between the U.S. and Ukraine and the coo

item.title: White House Faces Criticism for AI Video Depicting Iran Conflict Using Bowling Imagery
item.description: The Trump White House faced widespread backlash online after posting a controversial AI-generated video related to the ongoing conflict with Iran. The 34-second clip begins with footage of U.S. bowling star Pete Weber hitting a strike and transitions to an animation showing bowling pins representing Iranian officials being struck by a bowling ball decorated with the American flag. The video then shows real footage of Iranian targets being destroyed, accompanied by the caption 'STRIKE' with explosion and bald eagle emojis. Critics on social media condemned the video as trivializing the conflict, which has already resulted in seven U.S. service member deaths and approximately 140 injuries. Commenters expressed embarrassment and outrage, describing the video as 'cringeworthy,' 'absolutely shameful,' and inappropriate given the gravity of the situation. Many criticized the administration for reducing serious military action to a playful, cartoon-like representation. The incident highlights

item.title: Brent Crude Prices Rise Amid Iran-Related Shipping Disruptions and Global Market Volatility
item.description: Brent crude oil briefly surpassed $100 per barrel early Thursday, marking another significant surge just days after it neared $120 due to escalating tensions in the Middle East. Iranian attacks on commercial shipping around the Strait of Hormuz have intensified supply concerns, contributing to a 9% jump in oil prices. The U.S. benchmark crude rose about 4.5% to $91 per barrel, while Brent traded 5.3% higher at around $97. The conflict has disrupted oil fields and refineries in Gulf nations and temporarily halted cargo traffic through the strategic strait, which handles one-fifth of the world’s traded oil. In response, the International Energy Agency announced the release of 400 million barrels from emergency reserves—the largest ever—to stabilize markets, and the U.S. plans to release 172 million barrels from its Strategic Petroleum Reserve next week. Global financial markets reacted to the heightened uncertainty, with declines in major stock indexes such as the S&P 500, Dow Jones, DAX

item.title: Potential Layoffs and Temporary Suspensions Loom in Spain's Henares Corridor Logistics Hub Amid Iran War and Hormuz Disruptions
item.description: An article from El Periódico de la Energía highlighted the severe risks to Spanish industry from the ongoing war against Iran, which has disrupted the Strait of Hormuz—a critical chokepoint for about one-fifth of global LNG and significant oil flows. Although Spain imports little energy directly from the region, the global domino effect has driven up energy costs, freight rates, and shipping delays. The Corredor del Henares, a key logistics corridor serving Madrid and one of Spain's largest, faces acute vulnerability. This area hosts over 43,000 jobs in Guadalajara province alone, with high concentrations of logistics, pharmaceutical, and temperature-controlled supply chains reliant on timely Asian imports. Massive port arrivals in Valencia and Barcelona, combined with pre-existing near-capacity operations, exacerbate delays of 2-3 weeks or more. The government is considering reactivating ERTEs (temporary employment regulation files) for productive reasons, leading to reduced hours and

item.title: Trump Endorses Brandon Herrera After Tony Gonzales Withdraws Amid Ethics Investigation
item.description: President Donald Trump has endorsed Republican Brandon Herrera for Texas’ 23rd Congressional District following the withdrawal of his primary rival, Rep. Tony Gonzales, due to an ethics investigation. Gonzales, who admitted to an extramarital affair with a former staffer, bowed out of the race after advancing to the GOP primary runoff. Herrera, a self-described Second Amendment activist and social media personality, previously narrowly trailed Gonzales in the primary with 43.33% of the vote compared to Gonzales’ 41.73%, triggering a runoff since neither candidate reached 50%. Trump's endorsement highlights Herrera as a pro-MAGA candidate, emphasizing policies such as economic growth, tax and regulation cuts, American energy dominance, election security, school choice, border security, and support for military, veterans, law enforcement, and Second Amendment rights. Herrera expressed gratitude for the endorsement and support from other conservative lawmakers, including Reps. Lauren Boeb

item.title: Memorable and Bold Fashion Moments on the Oscars Red Carpet
item.description: The Oscars are renowned for celebrating excellence in film, but the red carpet preceding the ceremony is a showcase of celebrity fashion daring and creativity. From Bjork's famous swan dress to Celine Dion's backward suit and Kristen Stewart's short shorts, the red carpet has become a stage for risk-taking and trend-setting. Fashion at the Oscars is not just about looking good—it’s about making a statement, drawing attention, and sometimes challenging conventional norms. Celebrities have the opportunity to leave a lasting impression in popular culture, and over the years, many have done so by breaking the usual rules of red-carpet attire. As anticipation builds for the 2026 Academy Awards, Sky News highlights some of the most unforgettable and conversation-starting looks, celebrating those who pushed boundaries and took fashion risks that remain iconic today.

item.title: Hornwort Protein Could Improve Photosynthesis Efficiency in Crops
item.description: Researchers have discovered a unique molecular feature in hornwort plants that could help increase crop efficiency. The small protein component, named RbcS-STAR, modifies the key photosynthesis enzyme Rubisco, causing it to cluster into dense structures within plant cells. Rubisco is essential for capturing carbon dioxide during photosynthesis but is naturally slow and prone to interacting with oxygen, which reduces plant growth efficiency. By forming these concentrated compartments, Rubisco operates more efficiently. Experiments showed that introducing RbcS-STAR into other plants, including Arabidopsis, triggered the same clustering effect, suggesting the protein could function across multiple plant species. This discovery offers a potential path to engineering crops like wheat and rice to improve photosynthesis and increase yields. While the clustering mechanism shows promise, researchers caution that plants must also efficiently deliver carbon dioxide to Rubisco for full effectivene

item.title: GFiber and Astound Broadband Announce Merger Backed by Stonepeak
item.description: GFiber, formerly known as Google Fiber, and Astound Broadband have announced plans to merge in a deal supported by infrastructure investor Stonepeak Infrastructure Partners. Under the agreement, Stonepeak will hold the majority ownership of the combined company, while Alphabet will retain a significant minority stake. Stonepeak previously partnered with Patriot Media to acquire Astound in 2020 for $8.1 billion, making it the largest investor in Astound. The merger is expected to finalize in the fourth quarter of 2026, and the combined business will be managed by GFiber's current executive team, led by CEO Dinni Jain. Jain, who became GFiber's CEO in 2018 and has prior experience with Time Warner Cable and Insight Communications, stated that the merger supports GFiber's goal of redefining internet connectivity and achieving operational and financial independence. The new capital and strategic focus are intended to accelerate GFiber's growth, expand its customer-first approach, and advan

item.title: Jake Paul Appears on Stage With Donald Trump Amid Wardrobe Mishap
item.description: Jake Paul joined former U.S. President Donald Trump on stage at a campaign rally in Hebron, Kentucky, on Thursday evening. The event, held at the Verst Logistics facility, featured Trump praising his allies and engaging with supporters wearing red campaign hats. During the rally, Trump invited Paul on stage, calling him a “courageous guy” and “a hell of a fighter,” while suggesting that he could consider a future in politics. Paul, who had publicly supported Trump in 2024, thanked the former president and spoke about the influence Trump had on his life. However, the attention quickly shifted from his speech to a visible wardrobe issue. Paul wore a light tan suit with a white shirt and tie, which appeared slightly ill-fitting, and noticeable sweat stains formed under his arms and spread through his jacket. The incident drew widespread attention on social media, with many users commenting on the irony of Paul experiencing a sweat issue despite his own deodorant brand, “W,” launched in 20

item.title: Clinical Trial Finds Sulthiame Reduces Breathing Pauses in Sleep Apnea Patients
item.description: A European clinical trial led in part by the University of Gothenburg has found that the drug sulthiame can significantly reduce breathing interruptions in people with moderate to severe obstructive sleep apnea. The study, published in The Lancet, involved 298 participants across four European countries using a double-blind design. Patients taking higher doses of sulthiame experienced up to 47% fewer pauses in breathing and showed improved overnight oxygen levels. Sulthiame works by stabilizing the brain's control of breathing, reducing the likelihood of upper airway collapse, the main cause of obstructive sleep apnea. Most side effects were mild and temporary. The findings offer hope for a pill-based alternative to continuous positive airway pressure (CPAP) machines, which many patients struggle to tolerate. Obstructive sleep apnea affects millions worldwide and is linked to increased risks of high blood pressure, cardiovascular disease, stroke, and type 2 diabetes. While CPAP remains

item.title: Pakistan Begins Naval Escort Operation Amid Energy Supply Concerns
item.description: The Pakistani government has launched Operation Muhafiz Ul Bahr, or 'Protector of the Seas,' aimed at safeguarding maritime transport and national trade amid disruptions to energy imports caused by ongoing regional conflicts. Pakistan relies heavily on natural gas from Qatar and crude oil from Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates, and current shortages have left the country with less than two weeks of oil reserves, while liquefied gas supplies are expected to run out by the end of March. The operation involves deploying Pakistani naval vessels to escort commercial ships through strategic waterways, including the Strait of Hormuz, highlighting Pakistan's balancing act between maintaining ties with the West and preserving friendly relations with Iran. Tehran faces uncertainty over the Pakistani naval presence, as Pakistan is traditionally considered an ally but also maintains close connections with the United States. Past tensions, such as Iranian airstrikes against Baluchi groups i

item.title: Pelosi Endorses Harry Dunn Against Hoyer's Candidate in Maryland Congressional Race
item.description: Former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi has endorsed Harry Dunn, a former U.S. Capitol Police officer who defended the Capitol during the January 6, 2021, riot, for a congressional seat in Maryland's 5th District. Dunn, previously backed by Pelosi in his unsuccessful 2024 congressional bid, is running to replace retiring Representative Steny Hoyer, Pelosi's longtime colleague and occasional rival. Hoyer supports his former campaign manager, state Delegate Andrea Boafo, creating a notable intra-party clash in their shared home state. Pelosi praised Dunn as a 'true American hero' who 'bravely defended our democracy from Donald Trump's violent MAGA mob' and continues to protect Americans from extremists like Trump. This endorsement reignites tensions between Pelosi and Hoyer, who previously backed different candidates in Maryland's 2022 gubernatorial race. Dunn expressed gratitude for Pelosi's support, highlighting her decades-long fight for democracy, which aligns with his own post-January 6 a

item.title: Iran's leadership shifts toward nuclear weapons amid escalating tensions
item.description: Iranian leaders, historically divided on pursuing nuclear weapons, have now reached a consensus in favor of developing them following recent aggressive actions by Israel and the United States. Diplomatic negotiations with the U.S. are widely perceived by Iranian hardliners as ineffective, having failed to ease international sanctions or prevent large-scale military threats. The appointment of Mojtaba Jamenei, a staunch hardliner, reflects this strategic pivot. The article suggests that Iran sees the North Korean model as the only viable path to national survival in the face of perceived imperialist aggression. It argues that while Israel and the U.S. publicly oppose Iran's nuclear ambitions, their own nuclear capabilities serve as a deterrent and a double standard. This shift could potentially undermine international non-proliferation efforts and escalate regional tensions in the Middle East. The piece presents a perspective that Iran’s nuclear development is a defensive response rathe

item.title: US Relocates KC-135 Aerial Refueling Tankers from Prince Sultan Air Base Amid Iranian Attacks
item.description: The United States has begun relocating its Boeing KC-135 Stratotanker aerial refueling aircraft from Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia, marking a tactical adjustment to its military presence in the Persian Gulf. This decision follows a series of repeated Iranian ballistic missile and drone strikes on the base and other regional U.S.-affiliated facilities, which have exposed significant vulnerabilities in the multi-layered air defense networks protecting Gulf states. These attacks have highlighted risks to critical logistics assets, as aerial refueling tankers serve as a key enabler for extended-range U.S. and allied air operations, including long-distance strikes. The repositioning—reportedly moving some tankers to European bases—coincides with evolving tactics, such as Israel's more frequent employment of standoff weapons like the Rampage missile, which can be launched from outside Iranian airspace to minimize exposure. Analysts view Iran's strategy as focused on sustained asymme

item.title: UK Regulators Demand Stronger Online Protections for Children from Major Tech Platforms
item.description: Following a parliamentary vote rejecting a blanket ban on social media for under-16s, UK regulators Ofcom and the Information Commissioner's Office (ICO) have intensified pressure on major tech platforms to ensure the safety of children online. Facebook, Instagram, Roblox, Snapchat, TikTok, YouTube, and X (formerly Twitter) have been asked to provide detailed explanations of their age verification systems, measures to prevent grooming, and policies addressing harmful algorithms. Ofcom has emphasized that product testing on children must stop and will publish public reports in May, with potential enforcement action if companies fail to meet standards. The ICO also warned high-risk platforms that additional regulatory measures could follow if child safety is not adequately addressed. Research indicates that existing minimum age policies are widely ignored, with 72% of children aged 8–12 accessing platforms meant for older users. Tech companies defended their efforts, citing AI-based age

item.title: Kristi Noem Loses Complimentary Mar-a-Lago Membership After Removal as Homeland Security Secretary
item.description: Former South Dakota Governor and ex-Department of Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem has reportedly lost her complimentary membership at Donald Trump's exclusive Mar-a-Lago club in Palm Beach, Florida, shortly after her abrupt dismissal from the DHS role in early March 2026. According to sources cited by columnist Rob Shuter in his Naughty But Nice Substack on March 10, the revocation stems from her fall from favor in Trump's inner circle following her exit from the administration. Palm Beach insiders noted that such perks, including free access to the private club, are typically extended to high-ranking allies only while they remain in good standing and in key positions. Noem, now reassigned as Special Envoy for The Shield of the Americas, would need to pay a $1 million initiation fee plus around $20,000 in annual dues to regain membership if she chooses to apply. The report describes Noem as 'completely blindsided' by her DHS removal, believing she was among Trump's most loyal s

item.title: Sixth Container Ship Damaged in Suspected Attack Near Dubai Amid Iran Conflict
item.description: A container ship was struck by a suspected projectile off the coast of Dubai, marking the sixth such incident in recent days amid escalating tensions in the US-Israel-Iran war that began in late February 2026. The vessel, identified in related reports as possibly the Japanese-flagged ONE Majesty, sustained damage approximately 25 nautical miles northwest of the UAE port of Ra's al Khaymah, according to British maritime authorities like UKMTO. All crew members were reported safe, with the extent of damage under investigation. This attack follows a pattern of strikes on commercial shipping in the Persian Gulf and Strait of Hormuz, where shipping has nearly halted, disrupting global oil flows that account for a fifth of world supply. Previous incidents include three ships hit near the Strait of Hormuz, including a Thai-flagged vessel with three missing crew, and attacks on tankers in Iraqi waters that killed at least one sailor. Suspected Iranian drones or explosive boats are blamed, para

item.title: American Visitor Shares Experience Discussing Politics in a Northern Ireland Pub
item.description: A TikTok creator known as @lawcroxnotn shared a video describing her experience as a Trump supporter visiting a pub in Northern Ireland. According to her, the atmosphere shifted when a local patron realized they were American, leading to questions about their political choices. The TikToker explained that she wanted to keep the evening light and avoid political discussions, particularly in a social setting where people were drinking. She clarified that no one directly targeted her or her husband, but the environment became tense due to news discussions and the patrons’ reactions. The video sparked widespread online discussion and criticism, particularly from other TikTok users such as @pottymouthpollyanna, who argued that being asked about voting preferences does not constitute being shamed or victimized. This exchange led to a broader debate about politics, travel, and how Americans’ political choices are perceived abroad. The incident highlights the challenges of discussing politics

item.title: Iran Confirms Withdrawal from 2026 World Cup Amid Political Tensions
item.description: Iran's participation in the 2026 World Cup has been officially ruled out by the country's sports minister, Ahmad Donyamali. This comes amid escalating regional tensions following attacks by US and Israeli forces on Iran and subsequent Iranian retaliation. Despite FIFA president Gianni Infantino meeting with US President Donald Trump, who assured that Iran would be welcome to compete, Iran's leadership has deemed participation impossible, citing the assassination of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and the unsafe environment for their national team. The Iranian Football Federation head, Mehdi Taj, questioned the prudence of sending players into such a high-risk situation. The team was scheduled to play against New Zealand, Belgium, and Egypt in the tournament co-hosted by the US, Mexico, and Canada. Travel restrictions from the US will prevent Iranian fans from attending, although exemptions would allow teams to participate. Additionally, the situation for Iranian women's footballers remains tens

item.title: Jennifer Lopez Reveals She Took a Year Off Work to Process Her Divorce from Ben Affleck and Focus on Family
item.description: In a candid interview previewed on Good Morning America and set for Nightline, Jennifer Lopez opened up about the personal toll of her 2024 divorce from Ben Affleck. The 56-year-old entertainer described intentionally halting her career for a full year following the split, canceling planned tours to avoid using work as an escape. Instead, she chose to remain at home, fully confronting the emotional reality of the breakup without distractions from professional commitments, new relationships, or other avoidance tactics. Lopez emphasized the importance of self-reflection during this period, questioning her own patterns and role in the relationship's challenges rather than assigning blame elsewhere. She highlighted that her primary focus shifted to being present for her teenage twins, Emme and Max, whom she shares with ex-husband Marc Anthony, making them her top priority amid the turmoil. The decision stemmed from years of maintaining an intense, multifaceted career in music, film, and bu

item.title: Tina Wang Delivers an Emotionally Cohesive Piano Recital at Claremont United Church
item.description: On February 22, 2026, pianist Ching‑Ting (Tina) Wang presented a solo recital at the Sanctuary of Claremont United Church of Christ, focusing on a seamless emotional journey rather than separate showpieces. Her program progressed from Beethoven’s Classical clarity, through Brahms’ introspective warmth, to Chopin’s dramatic narrative, concluding with Ravel’s crystalline brilliance. Audience feedback highlighted her precise tone control, nuanced phrasing, and careful shaping of transitions, creating a deeply immersive listening experience. The sanctuary itself, designed by Theodore Criley Jr. in 1955 with Romanesque influences and local rock materials, enhanced the performance through its architectural resonance, stained glass, and acoustical qualities. The venue’s history of high-caliber performances, including renowned organists, further underscored the event’s prestige. Wang’s musical formation—including advanced degrees, international awards, festival participation, and pedagogical e

item.title: Congressional Powers and Oversight in Response to Military Action in Iran
item.description: Despite President Trump's large-scale military assault on Iran, the reasons provided for entering the conflict have been inconsistent, ranging from regime change and the destruction of nuclear weapons to preempting Israeli action or following a perceived divine plan. These justifications have significant constitutional implications, particularly regarding Congress's authority over war declarations. Under Article 1, Section 8 of the U.S. Constitution, only Congress can declare war, although the 1973 War Powers Resolution allows a president to engage in hostilities for up to 60 days without prior congressional approval if an 'imminent threat' exists. Trump’s administration has invoked this language to bypass initial congressional consent. Congress has responded through war powers resolutions and oversight measures, though partisan divides have limited their effectiveness. Democratic lawmakers have sought to introduce resolutions to curb military action, while some Republicans have suppor

item.title: Richard Kahn Testifies He Was Unaware of Jeffrey Epstein’s Full Abuse During House Deposition
item.description: Richard Kahn, who managed finances for convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein for over a decade, testified in a closed-door deposition before the House Oversight Committee that he did not know the full scope of Epstein’s sexual abuse until after Epstein’s death. Kahn, also a co-executor of Epstein’s estate, expressed regret over possibly assisting Epstein unknowingly and stated he would have stopped working for him had he been aware of the misconduct. Democrats on the committee criticized Kahn’s vague responses, questioning the credibility of his claimed ignorance, given his long involvement in managing Epstein’s financial operations, including payments that may have gone to victims. During the testimony, Kahn explained that he monitored Epstein’s gifts but did not see them as potential red flags for abuse. He also claimed no role in establishing Epstein’s numerous companies and did not perceive them as suspicious. Kahn provided information on Epstein’s wealth, mentioning prominent cli

item.title: Grammarly Discontinues AI Expert Review After Lawsuit Over Unauthorized Use of Names
item.description: Grammarly, owned by the tech company Superhuman, is facing a class action lawsuit after its AI-powered 'Expert Review' feature used the names and likenesses of authors, journalists, and other professionals without their consent. The tool, which has now been discontinued, presented editing suggestions as if they came from prominent figures such as Julia Angwin, Stephen King, and Neil deGrasse Tyson. Julia Angwin is the only named plaintiff, representing a class of individuals who claim that Grammarly misappropriated their identities for profit. The lawsuit argues that using people’s names and work without permission violates laws in New York and California, seeking damages exceeding $5 million. Angwin reported that the AI-generated advice was often inaccurate or counterproductive, highlighting ethical and practical concerns about AI-generated content. Superhuman executives have apologized and confirmed that the feature has been disabled while they reconsider how to safely and fairly imp

item.title: Kathie Lee Gifford Expresses Frustration with Length of LGBTQ Acronym While Affirming Love and Non-Judgment in Faith-Based Interview
item.description: In a recent interview with conservative commentator Tomi Lahren on her Fox Nation show, retired television host Kathie Lee Gifford discussed balancing her Christian faith with acceptance of the LGBTQ community. Responding to a question about reconciling her beliefs with support for LGBTQ individuals—prompted by actor Shia LaBeouf's recent controversial remarks and arrest involving homophobic slurs—Gifford emphasized love as her guiding principle. She quoted the Bible's call to 'love your neighbor as yourself' and shared a personal message she said came from God: to stay too busy loving people she disagrees with to judge them. Gifford stated she has many gay friends and does not judge others, leaving that to God. However, she interrupted her thoughts to complain about the evolving LGBTQ acronym, saying she no longer knows how many letters it includes and urging, 'They’ve really got to stop with that. We know what you mean.' The comment came amid her promotion of her new book, a fictiona

item.title: Israel Conducts Airstrikes on Hezbollah Positions in Lebanon
item.description: Israel has launched a series of airstrikes targeting Hezbollah infrastructure in Lebanon amid escalating tensions in the region. The strikes, part of a broader response to ongoing threats, hit command centers, weapons storage sites, and other militant facilities in areas including Beirut's southern suburbs and southern Lebanon. This action follows Hezbollah's retaliatory rocket and drone attacks on Israel, prompted by recent high-profile assassinations and the expanding US-Israel conflict with Iran. Lebanese authorities report dozens killed, including civilians, and hundreds wounded, with tens of thousands displaced as evacuation orders are issued for areas near Hezbollah sites. The Israeli military describes the operations as precise and defensive, aimed at degrading Hezbollah's capabilities while warning civilians to avoid militant-linked locations. Hezbollah vows continued resistance, raising fears of a wider war. Lebanon's government distances itself, labeling Hezbollah actions ill

item.title: Pentagon Limits Press Access Over Photos of Secretary Pete Hegseth
item.description: The Pentagon has reportedly restricted press access to Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth due to concerns over 'unflattering' photographs. After a March 2 briefing—the first Hegseth attended since June 2025—photographers from Reuters, Getty, and the Associated Press captured images of the Secretary alongside Joint Chiefs of Staff chairman Gen. Dan Caine. Some of Hegseth’s staff were reportedly dissatisfied with how he appeared in the photos. Subsequently, certain photographers were barred from attending briefings on March 4 and 10, leaving only Defense Department staff photographers allowed. Pentagon press secretary Kingsley Wilson stated that the measure was intended to manage briefing room space and that photographs are released online for public use. The Department’s relationship with mainstream media has become increasingly strained under Hegseth, particularly due to preexisting restrictions from the Trump administration affecting journalists’ movements and interactions. HuffPost reque

item.title: UK Man Admits Encouraging Vulnerable American to Commit Suicide Over Video Call
item.description: A 21-year-old man from Morley, West Yorkshire, Dylan Phelan, has admitted to encouraging a vulnerable American man, Travis Dyer of Louisiana, to take his own life during a video call on October 30, 2024. Phelan, along with two others in the United States, urged Dyer to use a shotgun to commit suicide. Dyer, who had a history of trauma including the loss of his mother and younger sister in a car accident ten years prior, had been subjected to ongoing encouragement to self-harm from online groups on Discord. Phelan voluntarily went to the police in Leeds in March 2025, acknowledging his involvement and his attraction to 'darker' online communities. In addition to assisting suicide, Phelan was found in possession of indecent images of a child and extreme pornography, to which he pleaded guilty in separate hearings. Leeds Crown Court has adjourned sentencing until May 22, with Phelan being banned from using internet-capable devices until then. Prosecutors emphasized that Phelan actively pa

item.title: Meta Removes 10.9 Million Scam-Linked Accounts in 2025 and Introduces New Anti-Fraud Features
item.description:
Meta announced on March 11, 2026, significant steps to combat large-scale online scamming operations. In 2025, the company removed 10.9 million Facebook and Instagram accounts associated with criminal scam centers, a sharp increase from over 2 million in 2024. It also eliminated more than 159 million scam advertisements across its platforms. These efforts target organized 'scam compounds,' particularly in Southeast Asia, where coerced workers often run 'pig butchering' investment frauds and other schemes affecting victims globally, including in the US, UK, and Asia-Pacific regions.
A key highlight was Meta's collaboration with international law enforcement, including the Royal Thai Police, FBI, UK's National Crime Agency, Australian Federal Police, and others. This joint operation led to 21 arrests in Thailand and the disabling of over 150,000 accounts tied to Southeast Asian scam networks. The action built on a December 2025 pilot that removed 59,000 entities and issued arrest warran

item.title: Benefits of Using Plant-Based Soaps for Daily Skincare
item.description: Plant-based soaps have become increasingly popular among consumers seeking gentle, natural alternatives to conventional personal care products. Many traditional soaps contain synthetic additives and harsh chemicals that can cause dryness or irritation, making them unsuitable for sensitive skin. In contrast, plant-based soaps use natural ingredients like coconut, olive, and shea butter, which cleanse effectively while maintaining skin hydration and comfort. These soaps often feature simple ingredient lists, making it easier for consumers to understand what they are applying to their skin and reducing the risk of irritation. Additionally, natural fragrances from essential oils and botanical extracts provide subtle, pleasant scents without overwhelming artificial perfumes. Many people also choose plant-based soaps for their environmental benefits, as they are frequently made with sustainable, biodegradable ingredients and produced using eco-conscious methods. Overall, these soaps offer ba

item.title: How ISPs Enable Mass Surveillance in Spain
item.description: This article examines the role of Internet Service Providers (ISPs) in Spain as central actors in mass digital surveillance. By providing the routers and modems that connect households to the internet, companies like Movistar, Digi, Orange, and Vodafone gain access to virtually all online activity of their users. This positions ISPs as holders of highly valuable personal data, which can be exploited for both commercial and political purposes. A notable example is the 2023 European project TrustPid, also known as the 'European Supercookie,' designed to track mobile internet usage for targeted advertising. Unlike regular cookies, these trackers are embedded by the ISPs themselves, making them difficult for users to detect or remove. Legal frameworks, particularly Spain's Law 7/2021, further facilitate this surveillance under the guise of public safety and crime prevention. While formally aimed at criminal investigations, the law allows authorities to request almost any user data from ISP

item.title: Jersey investigates whether Abramovich's Chelsea sale proceeds may be proceeds of crime amid ongoing UK dispute
item.description: The frozen proceeds from Roman Abramovich's 2022 sale of Chelsea FC, totaling £2.407 billion, are under investigation in Jersey to determine if they constitute proceeds of crime, according to recent accounts from Fordstam Limited, the parent company during Abramovich's ownership. This probe, initiated by Jersey's attorney general in April 2022 as a money laundering inquiry into assets linked to Abramovich, focuses on funds from Camberley International Investments Limited, which holds a £1.429 billion interest-free loan claim against Fordstam. The investigation, referenced in November 2025 Jersey court judgments, adds complexity to efforts to release the money for humanitarian aid in Ukraine. Abramovich, sanctioned by the UK and Jersey in 2022 over his alleged ties to Vladimir Putin following Russia's invasion of Ukraine, has denied any wrongdoing and challenged the probes. The UK government, led by Prime Minister Keir Starmer, insists the full amount—around £2.5 billion—should go to a

item.title: South Carolina Measles Outbreak Shows Downward Trend After Reaching Nearly 1,000 Cases
item.description: A significant measles outbreak in South Carolina, the largest in the United States in over 30 years, is demonstrating signs of slowing after peaking in mid-January. The state has reported a steady decline in new infections, dropping to approximately 10 cases per week recently, compared to around 200 per week at its height. As of early March 2026, the outbreak—centered in Spartanburg County and sparked in October 2025—has approached 1,000 total cases, with more than 93 percent occurring in unvaccinated individuals, predominantly children under 18. Low vaccination rates in schools, holiday gatherings, and church events fueled rapid spread, leading to severe complications like pneumonia and encephalitis in some patients. State epidemiologist Linda Bell expressed cautious optimism about the downward trajectory but warned of risks from spring break travel and potential re-surges, urging continued vigilance. The outbreak prompted a substantial vaccination increase: Spartanburg County saw a 1

item.title: Netflix Acquires AI Startup InterPositive, Co-Founded by Ben Affleck, for Up to $600 Million
item.description: Netflix has reportedly acquired InterPositive, an AI-focused company co-founded by Ben Affleck, for a deal potentially worth up to $600 million. InterPositive develops tools that assist filmmakers in post-production, including tasks such as resolving continuity issues and enhancing scenes, but it does not create new content or use footage without authorization. While Netflix has not officially confirmed the financial terms, sources suggest that the upfront cash payment could be lower, with additional payouts linked to the company's performance targets. This acquisition aligns with Netflix's ongoing strategy to integrate artificial intelligence into content production, following its use of generative AI in original programming, such as creating complex scenes in the Argentine series “The Eternaut.” Other major streaming and media companies are also pursuing AI-driven production, with Amazon building internal AI teams for film and TV and Disney partnering with OpenAI. However, some indus

item.title: Florida Woman Charged with Attempted Murder After Shooting at Rihanna's Los Angeles Home
item.description: A 35-year-old woman named Ivanna Lisette Ortiz from Orlando, Florida, has been formally charged following an incident where shots were fired at the Los Angeles home of pop superstar Rihanna on Sunday afternoon, March 8, 2026. Authorities confirmed that Rihanna was present at the property along with her partner, rapper A$AP Rocky, their three young children, Rihanna's mother, and several staff members. Fortunately, no injuries were reported despite the gunfire targeting the residence, a trailer on the property where some family members were located, and even a neighboring home. Ortiz faces one count of attempted murder specifically directed at Rihanna, 10 felony counts of assault with a semiautomatic firearm covering Rihanna, A$AP Rocky, their children, staff, and neighbors, and three counts of shooting at an inhabited dwelling or vehicle. The charges stem from the use of a semiautomatic weapon in the attack. Ortiz appeared in court on Tuesday, March 10, where her attorney initially ent

item.title: Severe Tornadoes Cause Fatalities and Damage in Indiana and Illinois
item.description: Multiple severe storms swept across northwest Indiana and northern Illinois on Tuesday, producing tornadoes that caused significant destruction and claimed at least two lives. In Lake Village, Indiana, an elderly couple was killed when a tornado struck their home. Several residents were trapped in damaged houses, and emergency crews rescued them amid widespread destruction. Over 70 utility poles were knocked down, and numerous roads became impassable. Some residents, like David Ferris, sheltered in bathtubs while assisting with rescue efforts. Essential infrastructure, including a Family Dollar store and a gas station, was destroyed, and many large trees were uprooted. In Kankakee, Illinois, tornadoes were accompanied by record-sized hailstones up to six inches in diameter, potentially setting a new state record. The storms left thousands without power, although utility companies have restored service to most affected areas. Authorities urged residents to avoid travel while checking on

item.title: Global Oil Shortage Persists Despite Unprecedented IEA Stockpile Release
item.description: The International Energy Agency (IEA) has announced an unprecedented emergency release of oil from the stockpiles of its member countries, totaling 400 million barrels, in response to recent disruptions in the Persian Gulf. Despite this historic measure, oil prices have remained high, with Brent crude still approximately 25% above pre-crisis levels. Analysts explain that the release is unlikely to meet global demand because the world is facing a daily shortfall of around 15 million barrels. The IEA's release may cover only 4-5 million barrels per day, leaving a significant gap. Additional oil supplies could come from Saudi Arabia and the UAE through alternate pipelines, and limited shipping through the Strait of Hormuz might add another 0.5-1 million barrels. Even in the best-case scenario, a 4 million barrel daily shortfall would persist. This ongoing energy gap is affecting economies worldwide: in Asia, Indian refineries are closing, LPG supplies are rationed, and workers in Thailand

item.title: WhatsApp Introduces Parent-Managed Accounts for Children Under 13
item.description: Meta, the company behind WhatsApp, has announced a new feature that permits children under the age of 13 to use the messaging platform, but only through parent-managed accounts. Parents will create and supervise these accounts by linking them to their own, enabling them to control contact lists, group memberships, and privacy settings. These restricted accounts limit usage to messaging and calls, excluding features like Meta AI, statuses, channels, and disappearing messages in one-to-one chats. End-to-end encryption remains in place, meaning parents cannot view message contents, but they receive notifications for certain activities, such as group size increases or changes to disappearing message settings. The accounts stay linked until the child turns 13, with parents able to extend supervision for up to an additional 12 months. Meta states this responds to parental requests for safer access, as many under-13s already use the app informally. When underage users are detected, their acco

item.title: Riverside Police Department Moves to Terminate Three Officers Over Use of 100% Disabled Veteran License Plates Amid Discrimination Lawsuit
item.description: Three Riverside Police Department officers—Timothy Popplewell, Richard Cranford, and Raymond Olivares—are facing termination after the city refused to settle their lawsuit alleging discrimination based on veteran and disability status. The officers, all military veterans who joined the department in 2019, hold 100% disability ratings from the U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs, qualifying them for special California DMV license plates that provide benefits like fee exemptions and disabled parking privileges. These ratings can result from combined partial disabilities, such as PTSD, hearing loss, or back injuries, rather than total physical incapacity. The department placed them on administrative leave in May 2025 and launched an internal investigation after discovering the plates on their personal vehicles. Their attorney, Matthew McNicholas, argues the department wrongly assumes the plates indicate fraud or inability to perform duties, claiming the push for termination stems from con

item.title: Replit Raises $400 Million in Series D at $9 Billion Valuation
item.description: Replit, the AI-powered platform enabling 'vibe-coding' where users describe app ideas in natural language for AI to build them, announced a major funding milestone on March 11, 2026. The company secured $400 million in a Series D round, achieving a $9 billion post-money valuation. This marks a triple increase from its $3 billion valuation in September 2025, when it raised $250 million. The round was led by Georgian Partners, with participation from G Squared, Prysm Capital, Coatue, Andreessen Horowitz, Craft Ventures, Y Combinator, Accenture Ventures, Okta Ventures, Databricks Ventures, and celebrity angels Shaquille O’Neal and Jared Leto. Replit's growth stems from shifting focus to non-coders, allowing broader adoption through AI agents that turn ideas into deployable applications. Previously, in the 2025 round, Replit reported trajectory toward $150 million ARR. No updated ARR was shared here, but the company aims for $1 billion ARR by year-end 2026, signaling confidence in the expl

item.title: NTSB: Driver Distraction Preceded Fatal 2024 Ford BlueCruise Crashes
item.description: The National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB) released documents on March 11, 2026, detailing two fatal crashes in 2024 involving Ford Mustang Mach-E vehicles using the BlueCruise hands-free driving system. In the first incident, on February 2024 in San Antonio, Texas, a driver traveling at 74 mph on Interstate 10 struck a stationary 1999 Honda CR-V, killing its driver. Ford's driver monitoring system showed the Mach-E driver looking at the infotainment screen in the five seconds before impact, with only brief glances at the road. He received alerts to refocus but did not brake. The driver reported using navigation to a charging station. A photo captured two seconds prior showed him upright but facing slightly right. In the second crash, March 2024 on Interstate 95 in Philadelphia, a 23-year-old driver, Dimple Patel, hit a stopped 2012 Hyundai Elantra at 72 mph in a 45 mph construction zone, also striking a 2006 Toyota Prius. Both stopped drivers died; Patel, who was intoxicated and

item.title: Multinational Poll Shows Mixed Global Views on US Reliability, Strength, and Tech Leadership in Early 2026
item.description: A large-scale multinational poll conducted by Public First from February 6-9, 2026, surveyed over 10,000 adults across the United Kingdom, United States, France, Germany, and Canada to gauge perceptions of the United States. The survey, weighted for demographics and politics, reveals a complex picture of America's global image among key allies. The US receives strong recognition for its military power, with agreement rates of 79-85% across countries that it possesses a strong military. Economic perceptions are moderately positive in most places, though less enthusiastic outside the US itself. However, views on shared values, democracy promotion, and reliability as an ally are notably divided or negative in European countries and Canada. In the UK, only 34% view the US as a reliable ally, with higher skepticism among certain regions and demographics. Canada shows particularly low trust, with just 22% agreement on reliability and over half disagreeing on shared values. France and Germany

item.title: Rising Number of Student Protests Against Immigration Enforcement in U.S. Schools
item.description: A growing wave of student protests has been recorded across the United States targeting Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). According to Defending Education, an organization monitoring schools, there have been 334 strikes and protests so far this year, with at least 306 directed against immigration authorities. The trend, which dates back to 2022, spans 236 school districts in 48 states and the District of Columbia. Colorado’s Poudre School District has seen particularly high participation, with nearly 2,000 students joining a series of strikes in early February, including events at Fort Collins, Boltz, Webber, Preston, Lincoln, and Rocky Mountain high schools. In Washington, D.C., over 1,000 students from 15 schools marched to the Lincoln Memorial in late February, protesting immigration enforcement and carrying signs calling for family unity and resistance against ICE. Similar demonstrations occurred in Los Angeles, where around 200 students gathered at City Hall. Defending Ed

item.title: IEA Coordinates Record Oil Reserve Release Amid Middle East Supply Disruption
item.description: The International Energy Agency (IEA) announced a record coordinated release of 400 million barrels of strategic oil reserves to stabilize global supply chains disrupted by the conflict in the Middle East. The UK, one of 32 member states participating, will contribute 13.5 million barrels as part of this sixth emergency release since the IEA’s founding in 1974. The move follows attacks on energy infrastructure by Iran after U.S. and Israeli military strikes on Tehran, which have severely limited exports through the vital Strait of Hormuz. Typically responsible for handling around 20% of global energy shipments, the strait’s effective closure has reduced global crude and refined product exports to less than 10% of pre-conflict levels. Despite the unprecedented scale of the release and coordination with G7 nations, the impact on oil prices has been muted. Brent crude eased slightly to around $90.63 a barrel following the announcement, down from highs above $118 earlier in the week. Imple

item.title: Allegations of Regional False Flag Operations in the Gulf and Europe
item.description: According to the report, Israel is allegedly increasing operations described as false flag attacks targeting several countries in the Gulf region and parts of Europe. Incidents mentioned include attacks on Saudi Arabia's Ras Tanura oil complex using unidentified projectiles and drones, which Iran denies involvement in. Iranian officials attribute these attacks to Israel, claiming they are intended to mislead the public and strain Iran's relations with neighboring countries. Similarly, attacks involving drones near Azerbaijan, Cyprus, and British military bases in Cyprus have sparked accusations and denials from multiple nations. Iran and its military leadership have denied launching any missiles or drones toward Turkey, Azerbaijan, and Cyprus, placing blame on Israel instead. Former Qatari Foreign Minister Hamad Bin Jassim warned of a covert plan allegedly involving the United States and Israel to manipulate Gulf countries. He emphasized the need for Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) memb

item.title: Why Glass Leads as the Ultimate Material for Sustainable and Elegant Packaging
item.description: The article explores how the modern design world is embracing sustainability and timeless aesthetics, with glass emerging as a leading material in eco-friendly packaging. It argues that today’s consumers and designers expect packaging to reflect both environmental responsibility and visual sophistication. Glass captures this balance through its durability, purity, and elegance. Its chemical inertness makes it ideal for preserving high-end goods such as perfumes, skincare products, and beverages without contamination or degradation. Beyond its use, glass supports the circular economy through infinite recyclability and creative upcycling. Empty glass bottles often gain new life as décor, from minimalist vases to artistic containers, reducing waste while maintaining aesthetic value. The piece also examines how glass’s unique optical and tactile qualities enhance brand perception. Its light refraction, weight, and cool texture convey luxury and authenticity that plastic counterparts cannot

item.title: China Restricts OpenClaw AI Use in Government and Financial Institutions Over Security Risks
item.description:
Chinese authorities have begun restricting the use of OpenClaw AI applications within state-owned enterprises and government agencies, including major banks, due to concerns over data security and external communication risks. According to Bloomberg, official notices have instructed employees not to install or run OpenClaw software on office computers, with some bans extending to the personal devices of staff linked to state networks and even to families of military personnel. The move comes amid growing domestic fascination with ‘agentic’ AI systems like OpenClaw, which can perform tasks autonomously by accessing and transmitting large amounts of data — capabilities that raise cybersecurity alarms.
The government has not imposed a complete prohibition nationwide but is mandating prior approval for institutional use. Nonetheless, the decision reflects Beijing’s cautious stance toward privately developed AI tools that demand deep system permissions. At the same time, Chinese tech giant

item.title: China Reports Strong Trade Growth in Early 2026 with Rising Exports and Imports
item.description: China's foreign trade started 2026 with strong momentum, showing double-digit growth in the total value of merchandise trade. Between January and February, the total trade value surged 18.3 percent year-on-year, reaching approximately $1.12 trillion. Exports rose 19.2 percent year-on-year to 4.62 trillion yuan, while imports increased 17.1 percent to 3.11 trillion yuan. High-tech and high-value-added mechanical and electrical products led exports with a 24.3 percent increase over the same period. By region, the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) remained China's top trading partner, with trade exceeding 1.24 trillion yuan, up 20.3 percent. The European Union followed with 998.94 billion yuan in trade, a 19.9 percent increase. Trade with Latin America and Africa grew 19.7 percent and 34.2 percent, respectively. In contrast, trade with the United States fell 16.9 percent to 609.71 billion yuan. Meanwhile, trade with countries participating in the Belt and Road Initiative reac

item.title: Foreign Hacker Accessed FBI Files Related to Jeffrey Epstein Investigation
item.description: In 2023, an unidentified foreign hacker managed to breach the FBI’s New York Field Office, targeting files associated with the Jeffrey Epstein investigation. According to Reuters, which cited sources familiar with the case and court documents, the breach exploited a vulnerability in a server at the FBI’s Child Exploitation Forensic Lab. This vulnerability was reportedly left open by an FBI special agent working on the case. The court documents indicate that the hacker accessed certain files related to Epstein, although the full extent of the compromise remains under investigation. The FBI confirmed that after the incident, the affected network was contained and access by the hacker was restricted, with the network later secured. Interestingly, the hacker reportedly did not realize they had infiltrated the FBI until agents contacted them via a video call and displayed official FBI credentials. The investigation into the breach is ongoing, with the bureau working to ensure no further una

item.title: Innovative Lunar New Year Packaging Turns Into a Horse-Racing Board Game
item.description:
Vietnamese design studio Witta Twist has created a visually striking and multifunctional project for Web3 company Ninety Eight, blending package design and interactive play in one concept. Named 'The Glorious Gallop,' the piece celebrates the Lunar New Year through both its artistic design and inventive structure. The gift box is built around the horse motif, symbolizing energy and prosperity for the new year. Its design relies on sharp geometric shapes, dynamic speed-line textures, and a vivid combination of red, yellow, and magenta, evoking the spirited atmosphere of celebration.
The real innovation lies inside the box. It unfolds into a complete, functional horse-racing board game, seamlessly transforming the packaging into the playing surface itself. Every detail — from the translucent game pieces and color-coded tracks to matching red envelopes — contributes to a unified visual and thematic experience. Rather than being a decorative box that happens to include a game, 'The Glorio

item.title: YouTube Launches Pilot Program for Deepfake Detection Among Public Figures and Journalists
item.description:
YouTube has announced an expansion of its AI-powered deepfake detection tools, extending them to a select group of politicians, government officials, and journalists. The aim of this pilot program is to help these individuals identify and request the removal of AI-generated videos that impersonate them. This represents a new phase of a system that was initially made available in 2025 to around four million creators through the YouTube Partner Program. The technology functions similarly to YouTube’s Content ID system, which detects copyrighted material. The new feature uses AI to spot manipulated video content that fakes someone’s likeness, such as their facial features or movements. These deepfakes can potentially mislead audiences by making it appear as if public figures said or did something they never actually did.
YouTube’s Vice President of Government Affairs, Leslie Miller, explained that not all flagged matches will automatically be taken down. Instead, removal requests will b

item.title: Louis Theroux Explores the Influence and Profitability of the Manosphere Online
item.description: Louis Theroux, acclaimed documentarian known for exploring extreme and controversial subcultures, turns his focus to the online 'manosphere' in his new Netflix documentary, Inside the Manosphere. The manosphere encompasses a wide range of online influencers, including podcasters, fitness personalities, and crypto enthusiasts, but Theroux concentrates on its most extreme corners, where misogyny, racism, and manipulation are used to attract young male audiences. Theroux explains that these influencers exploit sensational content as a gateway to sell products or promote online courses, making it financially lucrative to be provocative and offensive online. Throughout the documentary, Theroux is also filmed by the influencers, highlighting the complex dynamics of media in the digital age. Notably, he was unable to secure participation from Andrew Tate, one of the movement's most recognizable figures. Theroux emphasizes that while some online figures perform their harmful behaviors for atte

item.title: Van Crashes Into White House Barricade, Driver Arrested, No Injuries Reported
item.description: Early on Wednesday morning in Washington DC, a van drove into a temporary security barricade near the White House. The incident occurred at approximately 6:30 a.m. local time at the intersection of Connecticut Avenue and H Street Northwest. Secret Service officers from the uniformed division promptly arrested the driver on the scene. Authorities reported that no injuries resulted from the crash. Following the event, a police bomb squad was dispatched to examine the vehicle for potential threats, ultimately confirming that it was safe. The driver is currently being interviewed by law enforcement, and criminal charges are pending.
