Officials have emphasized that public safety remains a priority while the investigation continues. This event has raised concerns about celebrity security and gun violence in residential areas.

item.title: US State Department Mandates Departure of Non-Essential Diplomats and Families from Saudi Arabia Amid Escalating Regional Conflict
item.description: On March 8, 2026, the U.S. State Department issued an ordered departure directive for non-essential U.S. diplomats, government employees, and their family members stationed in Saudi Arabia, citing increased safety risks stemming from the ongoing war involving the United States, Israel, and Iran. This mandatory evacuation builds on earlier voluntary departure approvals and follows a series of escalations in the region. The conflict intensified after U.S. and Israeli airstrikes on Iran began around late February 2026. In early March, specifically around March 3, Iranian drones struck the U.S. Embassy in Riyadh, causing a limited fire and minor damage to facilities, though no casualties were reported at the time. This attack prompted immediate shelter-in-place alerts and contributed to the decision to scale back diplomatic presence. The United States had already started pulling non-essential staff from other Gulf Arab countries days into the war. The move reflects growing concerns over po

item.title: Allegations of Religious Messaging to U.S. Troops Regarding Iran Conflict
item.description: According to reports compiled by the Military Religious Freedom Foundation (MRFF), over 100 complaints from U.S. troops suggest that some military commanders have been promoting a religious narrative tied to the Second Coming of Jesus in the context of the U.S. military actions in Iran. These allegations claim that personnel were told the war was part of a divine plan and even referenced the Book of Revelation. While the White House denies any religious motivation, emphasizing strategic objectives such as dismantling Iran's missile program and navy, the MRFF maintains that troops are being exposed to Messianic propaganda. Secretary of Defense Hegseth and other Christian nationalist figures are highlighted as influential in shaping this messaging. Critics argue that this reflects a blend of political and religious motivations, intertwining U.S. foreign policy with religiously framed narratives. The article also notes that underlying political alliances, particularly with Israel, are a s

item.title: Trump Administration Weighs Potential Special Forces Operation to Secure Iran's Highly Enriched Uranium Stockpile
item.description: According to a Bloomberg News report cited by multiple sources including Reuters and HuffPost, U.S. President Donald Trump is evaluating the deployment of special operations forces inside Iran to seize or secure the country's stockpile of near-bomb-grade uranium, enriched to levels close to weapons-usable (around 60% or higher). This consideration arises amid escalating U.S.-Iran conflict, following recent airstrikes on Iranian nuclear facilities under Operation Epic Fury, which damaged enrichment sites but left uncertainty about the location and status of approximately 440 kilograms of highly enriched uranium. Diplomatic officials briefed on the matter indicate that U.S. and Israeli teams are actively searching for the material and have developed contingency plans, including a potential ground raid by special forces if its position is confirmed. The proposal remains in the planning stage with no final decision or approval for action, and Trump has publicly stated that such a move woul

item.title: Pentagon’s Dispute with Anthropic Highlights Challenges for Startups in Defense Work
item.description: In March 2026, negotiations between the Pentagon and AI startup Anthropic over the use of its Claude technology collapsed, leading to the Trump administration labeling the company a supply-chain risk. Anthropic announced plans to challenge this designation in court. Around the same time, OpenAI finalized its own deal with the Department of Defense, prompting user backlash and mass uninstalls of ChatGPT, while Anthropic’s Claude gained traction on the App Store. The dispute draws attention to the complex dynamics of working with the federal government, particularly in defense-related projects. TechCrunch’s Equity podcast panelists, including Kirsten Korosec, Sean O’Kane, and Anthony Ha, noted that the heightened scrutiny is unusual because most companies working with the Pentagon, such as General Motors, operate under the radar. However, Anthropic and OpenAI’s high-profile consumer products amplified public attention, especially since the debate touches on military applications of AI, a

item.title: Jesse Jackson Jr. Reflects on His Father's Legacy, Highlights Disconnect with Former Presidents at Memorial
item.description: During the memorial service for the late Rev. Jesse Jackson in Chicago, his son, Jesse Jackson Jr., delivered a pointed message regarding the tributes given by former U.S. presidents Barack Obama, Bill Clinton, and Joe Biden. Jackson Jr. expressed that while the presidents spoke for hours, they did not truly know his father. He emphasized that Rev. Jackson’s life was defined not by political allegiances but by his commitment to marginalized communities, advocating for the disenfranchised, and maintaining a prophetic voice that consistently stood for social justice. The memorial was attended by notable political figures, including former Vice President Kamala Harris, former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton, and California Governor Gavin Newsom. Each former president addressed the audience, with Obama highlighting challenges to democratic institutions, Clinton recalling personal conversations during his impeachment, and Biden making a remark about his own intelligence while reflecting

item.title: Satellite Radar Monitoring Could Reduce High-Risk Bridge Classifications by Detecting Early Deformations Globally
item.description: Researchers from the University of Houston and international collaborators have demonstrated that satellite-based radar technology, specifically Multi-Temporal Interferometric Synthetic Aperture Radar (MT-InSAR), offers a powerful tool for monitoring the structural health of long-span bridges worldwide. In an analysis of 744 bridges published in Nature Communications, the study reveals that many bridges, particularly in North America (which ranks poorest in condition) and Africa, are aging beyond their original design life and face heightened risks from geo-hazards like subsidence and landslides. Traditional inspection methods—limited to visual checks a few times per year or expensive on-site sensors installed on fewer than 20% of such bridges—often miss early warning signs. MT-InSAR detects millimeter-scale movements from space, enabling more frequent, cost-effective, and broad-area surveillance using satellites like ESA's Sentinel-1 and NASA's NISAR mission. Integrating this data int

item.title: Palmer Luckey's ModRetro in Talks to Raise Funds at $1 Billion Valuation
item.description: ModRetro, the retro gaming company founded by Palmer Luckey, is reportedly in early discussions to raise new funding at a $1 billion valuation, according to sources cited by the Financial Times. Luckey, best known as the founder of Oculus VR and the defense technology firm Anduril Industries, launched ModRetro's first product in 2024: the Chromatic, a high-end, cartridge-based handheld device inspired by the classic Nintendo Game Boy. Reviews, including one from The Verge, praised it as potentially the best Game Boy version ever made due to its authentic design, modern upgrades, and faithful recreation of the original experience. Luckey has described the Chromatic as the culmination of nearly two decades of hobbyist work involving deliberate choices to preserve the console's nostalgic appeal. The company is now developing additional hardware, including a device aimed at replicating the Nintendo 64. The funding talks come amid Luckey's broader success with Anduril, which is reportedly p

item.title: Marjorie Taylor Greene Criticizes White House Over Hypothetical Military Draft Remarks
item.description: Former Congresswoman Marjorie Taylor Greene reacted strongly after White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt appeared on Fox News’ Sunday Morning Futures. During the interview, host Maria Bartiromo asked Leavitt about parental concerns regarding a potential military draft amid ongoing military actions in Iran. Leavitt responded that President Trump 'does not remove options off the table,' which some interpreted as leaving a draft possible. Far-right commentator Stew Peters further amplified this interpretation online, falsely claiming that Leavitt explicitly stated a draft might occur. The White House's Rapid Response team corrected this misinformation. Despite these clarifications, Greene publicly criticized the administration, demanding clear assurances against a draft and deployment of U.S. troops, citing Trump’s campaign promises of no more foreign wars or regime change. Greene emphasized her disapproval of any military escalation and repeatedly called out Trump for what she vie

item.title: Fox News Apologizes for Airing Old Footage During Trump’s Attendance at Military Dignified Transfer
item.description: Fox News admitted to mistakenly showing archival footage of President Donald Trump at a dignified transfer ceremony instead of the event that occurred on Saturday, March 7, 2026, for six U.S. service members killed in the war with Iran. During the ceremony, Trump wore a white baseball cap with 'USA' on it, which drew criticism from some who considered it disrespectful and lacking in dignity. On the following day, during an episode of 'Fox and Friends Weekend,' co-host Griff Jenkins addressed the error, explaining that the video aired was from a previous dignified transfer and was used inadvertently. Fox News Media released a statement expressing regret for the mistake, apologizing to viewers and extending condolences to the families of the deceased service members. The article emphasizes the network's acknowledgment of the error and the controversy surrounding Trump's attire at the ceremony, highlighting both journalistic accountability and public scrutiny of presidential conduct durin

item.title: Opinion: The Assault on Iran Stems Fundamentally from Israel's Conflict with Palestine
item.description: In this opinion piece, author Yakov Rabkin argues that the ongoing US-Israel war against Iran, which began with joint strikes on February 28, 2026, is not primarily about US interests or preventing an Iranian nuclear threat, but rather about Israel's long-standing goal to weaken or eliminate Iran as the last major regional power supporting Palestinian rights and resistance groups like Hezbollah and Hamas. Netanyahu, who has reportedly planned such a confrontation for decades, benefits regardless of the outcome for the US, as any weakening of Iran advances Israel's objectives. The author highlights Iran's principled support for Palestinians across sectarian lines, contrasts it with Arab states' normalization and collaboration with Israel via the Abraham Accords, and criticizes Western governments for echoing unsubstantiated claims about Iran's nuclear program. Rabkin links this to broader Zionist ideology, which prioritizes a Jewish nation-state over equality for all inhabitants between

item.title: US Justice Department Releases Previously Withheld FBI Interviews Alleging Trump Sexual Assault of Minor in Epstein Case
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice recently published additional documents from its files on convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, including FBI interview summaries (known as 302 reports) that had been withheld earlier. These documents, released around early March 2026, detail allegations from a woman interviewed multiple times by the FBI in 2019 following Epstein's arrest. The woman claimed that Epstein introduced her to Donald Trump in the 1980s, when she was between 13 and 15 years old. According to her account in the reports, during an encounter arranged by Epstein in New York or New Jersey, Trump attempted to force her to perform oral sex. She stated that she bit him in response, after which he allegedly struck her before ordering her removal from the room. The interviews occurred between July and October 2019, and the allegations remain uncorroborated, with no charges ever filed against Trump related to this claim. The release stems from ongoing transparency efforts under the Epste

item.title: Oil Prices Jump Over $100 per Barrel Amid Rising Tensions in Iran
item.description: Oil prices have risen above $100 per barrel for the first time since Russia’s 2022 invasion of Ukraine, driven by escalating conflict in Iran. US crude futures increased by 14.7%, while Brent, the global benchmark, climbed 12.63% to $104 per barrel. The surge in oil has had an immediate impact on US financial markets, with Dow futures dropping 851.6 points and S&P 500 and Nasdaq futures falling 1.73% and 1.65%, respectively. Gasoline prices in the US also rose sharply, averaging $3.45 per gallon, a 16% increase from the previous week. Concerns about prolonged disruption of Middle Eastern oil flows and potential retaliation by Iran, particularly around the strategic Strait of Hormuz, have fueled fears of inflation. The Trump administration has attempted to reassure the public, with President Donald Trump describing the price increase as a temporary “glitch” and Energy Secretary Chris Wright stating that US forces have no plans to target Iran’s energy infrastructure. However, Iranian off

item.title: Latino Voters Boost James Talarico in Texas Democratic Senate Primary
item.description: In the Texas Democratic Senate primary held on March 3, 2026, Latino voters in several majority-Latino counties showed unprecedented turnout for Democratic candidates, surpassing even the votes cast for Kamala Harris in the 2024 presidential election. State Representative James Talarico emerged victorious in a competitive primary, outperforming rivals like Jasmine Crockett by capitalizing on strong support from Hispanic communities along the border and in urban areas. This shift marks a potential reversal from 2024, when many Latino voters in Texas swung toward Republicans, including President Donald Trump, due to economic pressures and immigration concerns. Talarico's campaign emphasized working-class issues, education, and his Christian faith, resonating with blue-collar Hispanic families worried about rising costs and federal policies. In counties like those in the Rio Grande Valley, Democratic primary votes exceeded Harris's 2024 totals, signaling buyer's remorse among voters over

item.title: UK Surgeon Performs Long-Distance Robotic Prostate Surgery
item.description: In a groundbreaking medical development, a surgeon in London successfully performed the UK's first long-distance robotic surgery on a patient located 1,500 miles away in Gibraltar. Professor Prokar Dasgupta, a leading robotic urological surgeon, conducted a prostate removal operation on 62-year-old Paul Buxton using a robot equipped with a 3D HD camera and four robotic arms, all controlled via a console. The connection delay between the UK console and the Gibraltar robot was minimal, only 0.06 seconds, supported by fiber-optic cables and a backup 5G link. A local medical team in Gibraltar was on standby in case of technical issues, but the procedure proceeded smoothly without interruption. Buxton, diagnosed with prostate cancer, opted for the remote surgery to avoid long NHS waiting times, describing the experience as giving back to the medical community. This procedure demonstrates the potential for remote robotic surgery to reduce travel expenses for patients and increase access to s

item.title: Iran's Assembly of Experts Names Mojtaba Khamenei as Successor Amid Ongoing Conflict
item.description: In a pivotal development amid escalating conflict with the United States and Israel, Iran's Assembly of Experts has reportedly selected Mojtaba Khamenei, the second son of the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, as the new Supreme Leader. Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed in initial strikes launched under Operation Roaring Lion, prompting urgent deliberations within Iran's clerical leadership. Sources indicate the decision was influenced by pressure from the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC), which sought to expedite the succession outside standard procedures due to ongoing airstrikes that prevented a formal session. Mojtaba, aged 56, aligns with his father's hardline conservative stance, advocating suppression of domestic opponents and assertive foreign policies. He maintains close ties to the IRGC, despite lacking a prominent public profile or high religious credentials, which could complicate approval by the Guardian Council. Israeli officials anticipate a formal anno

item.title: White House Keeps Options Open on Possible Ground Deployment in Iran
item.description: During a recent Fox News appearance, White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt addressed questions about the potential deployment of U.S. ground troops in Iran. Leavitt emphasized that while no immediate plans are set, President Donald Trump is keeping all military options open. The discussion came shortly after Trump suggested the possibility of incorporating ground operations into Operation Epic Fury, though he stressed that such a decision would require strong justification. Leavitt also refuted an NBC News report claiming that Trump privately discussed a ground invasion with aides, clarifying that the report relied on anonymous sources outside the official national security team. Meanwhile, ongoing U.S. military actions in Iran have resulted in six service members killed and eighteen others injured since operations began last Saturday. The conversation also acknowledged public concerns, particularly from parents worried about a potential draft and the involvement of their childr

item.title: In-Depth Review: How the Capital One Venture X Rewards Card Offsets Its Annual Fee for Frequent Travelers
item.description:
The Capital One Venture X Rewards Credit Card is CNN Underscored’s top pick among premium travel rewards cards. Designed for frequent travelers, it offers strong earning potential, valuable credits, and luxury benefits that can offset its $395 annual fee. Cardholders earn 10 miles per dollar on hotels and rental cars, 5 miles on flights booked via Capital One Travel, and 2 miles on everyday purchases. A $300 annual travel credit and 10,000 anniversary miles help neutralize the fee, making it cost-effective for users who book travel regularly.
The card stands out for its robust lineup of perks: access to Capital One and Priority Pass lounges, Global Entry or TSA PreCheck credit, Hertz President’s Circle status, cell phone protection, and comprehensive travel insurance. Miles can be used either to erase travel purchases at a fixed rate of 1 cent per mile or transferred to over 15 airline and hotel partners for potentially higher value. CNN Underscored’s evaluation highlights the Venture

item.title: Startup Launches $70M Initiative to Automate Manufacturing Using AI
item.description: Bob McGrew, the former chief research officer at OpenAI and early Palantir employee, has co-founded a new startup called Arda, which has successfully raised $70 million at a valuation of $700 million. Arda is developing an advanced AI and software platform aimed at automating manufacturing processes. The platform includes a video analysis model that can interpret footage from factory floors and use the insights to train robots to operate autonomously. Arda’s software is designed to coordinate machines and humans across the entire production chain, from product design to finished goods. The startup’s goal is to enhance cost efficiency in Western manufacturing and reduce reliance on China amid rising geopolitical and national security concerns. McGrew’s experience at OpenAI focused on training robots for physical-world tasks, providing a foundation for Arda’s vision. The move highlights a growing trend of leveraging AI and robotics to modernize and potentially reshape global manufacturin

item.title: Democrats Target Latino Voters in Key Primary Races Across Swing Districts
item.description: Democrats are increasingly focusing on Latino voters following record turnout in recent primaries, particularly highlighted by James Talarico's strong performance in Texas. This surge has prompted campaigns in competitive districts from Colorado to Arizona and Nebraska to prioritize outreach to Latino communities, which could prove pivotal in flipping narrow Republican-held seats. In Colorado's 8th Congressional District, which is 39 percent Latino, candidate Rutinel emphasizes his background as the only Latino and bilingual contender, aiming to connect directly with these voters ahead of the June 30 primary. A potential victory there could lead to a Latino-versus-Latino general election matchup against incumbent Evans. Arizona's 6th District, while less Latino-dominated, remains influenced by the demographic, encompassing parts of Tucson, a major Air Force base, and border-area voters—one in three residents is Latino, according to strategists. Democratic operatives note the high stake

item.title: Democrat Talarico Faces Major Challenges in Texas Senate Race
item.description: Democratic candidate Talarico has recently gained momentum in Texas, signaling a potential opportunity for the Democratic Party to make inroads in a traditionally Republican stronghold. Despite this progress, the path ahead remains difficult, as Texas continues to present significant political challenges for Democrats. Analysts point to new openings for House wins in areas that have been solidly Republican, especially following the results of the first midterm races. The state’s Senate races are also in focus, with key developments such as GOP Representative Gonzales ending his reelection bid after admitting to an affair with a late staffer, and GOP Senator Steve Daines withdrawing from the Senate race to prevent Democrats from recruiting a top-tier challenger. Meanwhile, former President Obama has voiced support for Virginia's redistricting efforts as early voting approaches. On the Republican side, Governor Abbott is projected to win the GOP primary, while Texas GOP Senate primaries

item.title: US Veteran Explains Cartel Fragmentation and Risks Following Jalisco New Generation Leader’s Death
item.description: Nearly two weeks after Mexican authorities killed Ruben 'El Mencho' Oseguera Cervantes, the head of the Jalisco New Generation Cartel (CJNG), experts and former military personnel emphasize that the fight against drug cartels is far from over. Carlos De La Cruz, a 20-year U.S. Air Force veteran who served along the southern border, highlighted that while Oseguera's death represents a significant blow to one of the world's most violent cartels, the organization itself is unlikely to collapse. Instead, cartels tend to fracture, leading to short-term spikes in violence as factions compete for control of territory. De La Cruz stressed that dismantling the CJNG requires a sustained, multi-pronged approach, targeting both the cartel's financial networks and infrastructure, not just individual leaders. He also warned that the fentanyl crisis should be considered a domestic security emergency, as these substances continue to flow into the United States from Mexico. Following the cartel leader’

item.title: US Continues to Rely on Upgraded B-52 Bombers from the 1960s for Modern Conflicts, Including Strikes on Iran
item.description: The Boeing B-52 Stratofortress, specifically the H model introduced in 1961, remains a cornerstone of the United States Air Force's bomber fleet more than six decades later. With 76 aircraft still in active service, the B-52 accounts for half of the USAF's bombers and is frequently deployed for major operations. Known affectionately by crews as the 'BUFF' (Big Ugly Fat Fella), it boasts an impressive payload capacity of up to 70,000 pounds of conventional or nuclear weapons, including up to 20 cruise missiles, a range of 8,800 miles extendable via refueling, and a top speed of 650 mph. Operating costs are relatively low compared to newer bombers. The aircraft, originally designed for nuclear deterrence during the Cold War, has seen extensive conventional use in conflicts such as the Vietnam War (notably Operation Linebacker II), the Gulf War, the 2003 Iraq invasion, and operations against ISIS starting in 2014. Currently, under President Donald Trump, B-52s are actively involved in str

item.title: Josh Elliott and Liz Cho's Divorce Proceedings Intensify with Disputes Over Assets and Allegations
item.description: Former CBS anchor Josh Elliott filed for divorce from ABC News anchor Liz Cho on June 20, 2025, in Connecticut court, citing that their nearly ten-year marriage, which began in July 2015, has broken down irretrievably. Elliott seeks dissolution of the marriage and an equitable distribution of property. Cho responded on November 6, 2025, with her own cross-complaint echoing the irretrievable breakdown claim. The case has escalated into contentious discovery requests, with Cho seeking extensive communications, financial records, and details of any expenditures or relationships Elliott may have had with others since July 2015. Elliott objected to these as overly broad and irrelevant. Tensions rose further when Cho filed a contempt motion, alleging that on January 19, 2026, while she was away, Elliott removed significant furniture, furnishings, and their two Portuguese Water dogs from their jointly owned Connecticut home. She also claimed a valuable watch and earrings went missing from her

item.title: Matt Stevens Transforms Popular Movies into Vintage Book Covers
item.description: Artist and designer Matt Stevens combines his love for cinema with his graphic design skills in a creative project that reimagines well-known movies as vintage-style book covers. The series, called 'Good Movies as Old Books,' features iconic films such as 'Mad Max: Fury Road,' 'The Shawshank Redemption,' and 'Back to the Future,' reinterpreted with the aesthetic of classic paperback and hardcover designs. Stevens pays close attention to details, incorporating worn pages, creases, and other signs of aging to enhance the authenticity of the covers. This artistic endeavor not only celebrates these films but also captures the nostalgia and charm of mid-20th-century book design. Fans of both movies and design are encouraged to explore his work on Instagram and his website, where the full collection is showcased. Stevens’ project demonstrates how art can bridge different mediums, turning cinematic experiences into tangible, collectible objects while highlighting the timeless appeal of though

item.title: Study Finds Left-Handed People May Have Competitive Advantages
item.description: A recent study by researchers at the University of Chieti-Pescara in Italy explored how handedness may relate to competitiveness. The study found that left-handed individuals, especially men, tend to be more competitive in one-on-one scenarios, while right-handed people often exhibit better cooperative behavior. This phenomenon is explained through the concept of evolutionarily stable strategy (ESS), suggesting that left-handedness provides a minority advantage in competitive contexts due to its unpredictability. The research included two experiments: one with 1,100 participants completing questionnaires on handedness and competitiveness, and another with 48 participants performing a pegboard dexterity test. Results showed that left-handers scored higher on traits like hyper-competitiveness and personal development-oriented competitiveness, but hand preference did not correlate with motor skills. Additionally, the study found no significant differences between left- and right-handed in

item.title: Expert Analysis: Iran's Missile Stockpiles Remain Largely Intact in Underground Mountain Bases Despite US-Israeli Claims
item.description: The article counters claims by US and Israeli officials that Iran's missile arsenal is significantly depleted and that the number of launchers is low. According to Yury Lyamin, a researcher from Moscow's Center for Strategic and Technological Analysis, the total number of Iranian launchers is likely underestimated. While missile launch frequency has decreased, this stems from heightened caution due to ongoing US and Israeli strikes on tunnel entrances, which require time-consuming debris clearance and safety checks. Iran's primary missile reserves and launchers are stored deep underground in mountain facilities, making them extremely difficult to target or access. Iran uses simple, cost-effective designs for launchers, often mounted on standard trucks and trailers, allowing maintenance of a large inventory. Lyamin questions Israeli assertions of destroying 'more than half' of launchers, including a recent claim of 300 destroyed in two days, noting that videos show far fewer hits—possib

item.title: 'Survivor' Winner Richard Hatch Reflects on Negative Experiences with Donald Trump on 'The Celebrity Apprentice'
item.description: Richard Hatch, who won the inaugural season of 'Survivor,' publicly criticized former President Donald Trump, describing him as 'the worst human being' he has ever met. Hatch competed on the fourth season of Trump's 'The Celebrity Apprentice' and claimed he observed Trump's manipulative and self-serving behavior over a three-year acquaintance before the show. Hatch alleged that Trump made inappropriate sexual comments toward female contestants, including in front of his daughter, Ivanka Trump. Although Trump’s 2016 campaign denied these claims, Hatch emphasized that Trump's interactions were consistently driven by personal gain and attention, often manipulating contestants against one another. Additionally, the article notes prior allegations regarding Trump’s conduct on 'The Apprentice' and its spin-offs, including claims of racist language and objectifying remarks toward women. NBC eventually ended the Trump-hosted series after controversies over his rhetoric and public behavior. Hat

item.title: Study Links Receptivity to Corporate Buzzwords with Poorer Decision-Making and Analytical Skills
item.description:
A recent study from Cornell University, led by cognitive psychologist Shane Littrell and published in the journal Personality and Individual Differences, explores how susceptibility to corporate bullshit—vague, impressive-sounding jargon like 'synergistic leadership' or 'growth-hacking paradigms'—affects workplace performance. Littrell developed the Corporate Bullshit Receptivity Scale (CBSR) to measure this trait. Using a custom 'corporate bullshit generator' to create meaningless yet buzzword-heavy sentences, the researcher tested over 1,000 office workers by having them rate these statements' perceived 'business savvy' alongside genuine quotes from Fortune 500 executives.
Findings revealed a clear pattern: employees more receptive to such corporate BS tended to view their supervisors as more charismatic and visionary. However, they scored lower on measures of analytic thinking, cognitive reflection, fluid intelligence, and effective workplace decision-making. Paradoxically, these s

item.title: Reflections on Iran's Political Struggles and Civilian Suffering
item.description: The article is a personal reflection on the ongoing turmoil in Iran, particularly in light of recent violent attacks and political instability. The author describes anxiously tracking news updates about missile strikes, civilian casualties, and the death of Ayatollah Khamenei, while noting that mainstream media largely ignores the personal stories of ordinary families affected by the conflict. The piece traces historical roots of Iran's political issues, including the 1953 CIA-led coup that overthrew Prime Minister Mohammad Mossadegh and reinstated the Shah, leading to the 1979 Iranian Revolution and the establishment of the Islamic Republic. The article emphasizes that despite decades of oppression, Iranians continue to pursue education, resist internal crackdowns, and fight for a better future. The author warns that external interventions are unlikely to create meaningful regime change, as no organized opposition exists to replace the current power structures, notably the IRGC. The p

item.title: Black Democrats Criticize Crockett's Texas Senate Primary Loss
item.description: Black Democratic strategists, lawmakers, and activists expressed frustration after Texas Democrats rejected Rep. Jasmine Crockett as their Senate nominee, opting instead for state Rep. James Talarico in the March 2026 primary. Critics pointed to Crockett's campaign shortcomings, including a weak ground game, inadequate infrastructure, overreliance on social media rather than TV ads, and failure to mobilize voters effectively in Texas's vast media markets. Despite endorsements from figures like former Vice President Kamala Harris, Crockett's two-term congressional tenure and viral fame did not translate into victory. Texas Rep. Jolanda Jones bluntly stated, 'This L is on her,' emphasizing Crockett's lack of political savvy. Strategists warned that choosing an untested white candidate over a prominent Black woman could alienate Black voters, especially women, who are key to Democratic turnout. Talarico, a seminary student and educator with national profile from appearances on Joe Rogan a

item.title: Bert Voorhees and Community Activists Protest U.S. Actions in Iran
item.description: Bert Voorhees, a 70-year-old retired lawyer and teacher from northeast San Fernando Valley, is actively protesting U.S. military action in Iran, viewing it as a threat to American democracy. In a recent rally at Los Angeles City Hall organized by the Answer Coalition, Voorhees emphasized that citizens must participate beyond voting to safeguard democracy. He believes the current administration's actions, including the bombing of Iran, reflect disregard for Congress and the courts. Voorhees, along with activists like Catherine Arias, Denise Giardina, and Roseanne Constantino, participate in regular demonstrations and community efforts to protect rights and hold politicians accountable. They argue that civic engagement is necessary, even amid widespread political fatigue and feelings of helplessness. While Voorhees acknowledges setbacks in the fight for justice, he remains hopeful, citing examples like Minneapolis communities successfully resisting aggressive policies. His message unders

item.title: TEPCO to Conduct First Visual and Radiation Survey of Fukushima Reactor Pressure Vessel
item.description: Tokyo Electric Power Company (TEPCO) is preparing to launch a pioneering probe into the pressure vessel of Reactor No. 2 at its Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant. This initiative is part of the ongoing effort to locate and eventually retrieve the estimated 880 tons of nuclear fuel debris that remained following the 2011 earthquake and tsunami, which caused a triple meltdown. The probe will use a fiberscope equipped with a camera and radiation sensors, inserted through piping to examine the outer side of the stainless steel shroud surrounding the nuclear fuel. The objective is to visually inspect the interior, identify any debris, and measure radiation levels on a location-by-location basis to inform future debris retrieval methods. TEPCO officials have conducted extensive mock-up drills and will implement measures to prevent radiation exposure to workers. Although initially scheduled for 2024, the project was delayed due to technical challenges in developing the dosimeter-equipped

item.title: Democrats Welcome Kristi Noem's Removal as DHS Secretary, Demand Further Changes
item.description: President Donald Trump announced the removal of Kristi Noem from her position as Homeland Security Secretary, appointing Oklahoma Senator Markwayne Mullin as her successor. Democrats expressed strong approval of Noem's ouster, with figures like California Governor Gavin Newsom labeling her tenure as the most cruel in U.S. history and calling for the dismissal of other Trump aides such as Stephen Miller and Greg Vino. Illinois Governor JB Pritzker mocked Noem on social media, stating 'don't let the door hit you on the way out' and highlighting her legacy of corruption and chaos, including incidents where parents and children were reportedly gassed. Noem's departure follows months of bipartisan criticism over her alleged ties to Corey Lewandowski, labeling Minnesota protesters killed by federal agents as 'domestic terrorists,' and a controversial $220 million DHS ad campaign promoting voluntary deportation, which she claimed Trump approved but he later denied. House Minority Leader Hakee

item.title: AI Leaders Discuss Potential Government Involvement in AI Development
item.description: CEOs from major AI companies, including Palantir and OpenAI, have publicly expressed concerns about the possibility of the U.S. government nationalizing AI technologies. Palantir's CEO warned that if Silicon Valley's innovations threaten jobs or military interests, government intervention could be inevitable. OpenAI's Sam Altman also speculated that AGI development might be better handled as a government project and acknowledged he has considered potential nationalization scenarios. The Defense Department recently invoked the Defense Production Act with Anthropic, a move described by analysts as a form of 'soft nationalization,' highlighting strategic control over AI development. OpenAI maintains that it controls the deployment of its AI models, and both Altman and his team emphasize the importance of collaboration with government while preserving operational independence. Employees from OpenAI and Google have signed letters opposing AI use in mass surveillance or autonomous weaponry.

item.title: SpaceX and Industry Plans for Space-Based AI Data Centers
item.description: In early February 2026, Elon Musk redirected SpaceX's primary goal from Mars colonization to the Moon, while simultaneously announcing plans for a massive constellation of one million satellites dedicated to AI data processing. This move places SpaceX at the forefront of a growing trend, as other companies like Google, Amazon, and Chinese firms are exploring or proposing their own space-based data infrastructures. Google’s Project Suncatcher aims to deploy thousands of satellites in heliosynchronous orbit, achieving terabit-per-second data speeds, while Amazon’s TeraWave constellation will use LEO and MEO satellites to enhance high-bandwidth connectivity, potentially integrating data processing in the future. Despite abundant solar energy in space, significant challenges remain, particularly cooling systems, radiation shielding, and cost-effective satellite deployment. SpaceX hopes to leverage Starship's high launch cadence and potential lunar resource utilization to reduce costs. The

item.title: The Constitutional and Economic Impact of Trump’s Military Strikes in Iran
item.description: Since January 2025, the Trump administration has launched over 600 military strikes in countries including Iran, Yemen, Nigeria, and Venezuela, with additional threats against Greenland, Colombia, and Mexico. This pattern of preemptive military action, exemplified by Operation Epic Fury, bypassed Congress, limited public debate, and challenged constitutional norms. The article emphasizes the separation of powers in the U.S. Constitution, noting that only Congress has the authority to declare war while the president serves as commander-in-chief, a distinction increasingly blurred by unilateral executive actions. Financially, the strikes have already cost American taxpayers over $1 billion, with estimates suggesting potential total costs between $50 billion and $210 billion. Human costs are even more significant, as thousands of troops face danger overseas. Critics argue that these actions benefit the military-industrial complex, fueling defense contracts, reconstruction deals, and intel

item.title: Experts Propose Guidelines for Safe and Accountable AI Development
item.description: Amid growing concerns over unregulated artificial intelligence, a bipartisan coalition of experts has released the Pro-Human Declaration, outlining a framework for responsible AI development. Finalized before the recent Pentagon-Anthropic conflict, the declaration emphasizes the need to keep humans in charge, prevent power concentration, protect the human experience, preserve individual liberties, and hold AI companies legally accountable. Key recommendations include banning superintelligence development without scientific consensus and democratic support, requiring mandatory off-switches for powerful AI systems, and prohibiting self-replicating or autonomously improving architectures. The timing highlights the urgent need for regulation: the Pentagon labeled Anthropic a 'supply chain risk' after the company refused to grant unlimited military access, while OpenAI secured a separate, legally complex deal. Max Tegmark, an MIT physicist and declaration organizer, likens AI safety regulat

item.title: Swedish Students Create the World's Smallest 3D-Printed Beer Bottle
item.description: A team of five students from Sweden's KTH Royal Institute of Technology has broken the record for the world's smallest beer bottle. Using a high-precision 3D printer, the students produced a 4-millimeter-tall Carlsberg bottle, roughly the size of a sesame seed. This minuscule bottle holds just 0.00001057 centiliters of alcohol-free beer, meaning it would take about 5.37 million such bottles to fill a standard pint. The project began with a digital 3D model of the classic 33-centiliter Carlsberg bottle, and students Joel Bovin, Annie Nordström, Lucas Bergman, Linnea Isaksson, and Karin Hallgren carefully pushed the printer to its technical limits. After printing, the bottle's interior was cleaned with a microcannula and cured under UV light. Miniature artist Åsa Strand then added the cap, label, filling, and coloring, completing the tiny, fully detailed replica. The challenge originated from Carlsberg, who offered 10,000 SEK and a trip to their research lab in Copenhagen as prizes for s

item.title: Betty Yee Maintains Campaign for California Governor Amid Crowded Democratic Field
item.description: Betty Yee, former California state controller, continues her campaign for governor despite trailing in polls and fundraising. She emphasizes practical fiscal management, accountability, and hands-on experience in state government over flashy or celebrity-driven campaigns. Yee criticizes inefficiencies in Sacramento, such as unresponsive government offices, and stresses that voters should understand the state’s structural budget deficit before new programs are launched. Her platform focuses on responsible spending, potential tax increases on wealthy residents and corporations, and a commitment to responsive governance. Unlike billionaire or media-savvy competitors, Yee relies on a grassroots strategy, visiting all 58 counties and engaging directly with voters. She believes voters will eventually reward substantive leadership over performative politics. Despite facing low chances of leading the race currently, Yee remains confident that straight talk and practical expertise can resonate

item.title: Republican Jewish Coalition Backs Gallrein, Criticizes Massie's Stance on Iran Conflict
item.description: The Republican Jewish Coalition Victory Fund launched a 30-second ad on Thursday supporting Ed Gallrein, President Donald Trump's endorsed candidate in Kentucky's GOP primary against Rep. Thomas Massie. The ad accuses Massie of siding with Iran and radical leftists in Congress by opposing U.S. military action against the regime, portraying him as against Trump, the nation, and the military on key issues like the border and taxes. This marks an early use of the ongoing Iran war in primary attacks, despite polls showing voter fatigue with such conflicts. Massie faces Gallrein in May after clashing with Trump over the Epstein files release alongside Democrat Ro Khanna. Massie responded by defending his record, noting he supported Trump and JD Vance in 2024 to end 'forever wars' and now faces millions in attack ads for consistency. Recently, the House rejected a Massie-Khanna measure to force Trump to end Iran operations without congressional approval, though Massie called it a win for put

item.title: Tehran Reduces Motorists' Fuel Allowance Following Israeli Strikes on Oil Storage
item.description: Authorities in Tehran have implemented fuel rationing for motorists after Israeli airstrikes damaged oil storage facilities in the Iranian capital and nearby Alborz province. The Tehran governor announced that the daily fuel purchase quota using personal cards at gas stations has been reduced from 30 liters to 20 liters. This marks the first significant restriction on subsidized motor fuel since the onset of the ongoing conflict between Iran, Israel, and the United States. The attacks occurred late Saturday, igniting fires at refinery storage tanks in southern Tehran, with firefighting teams still working to contain the blazes. Governor Mohammad-Sadegh Motamedian emphasized that there is no actual fuel shortage, urging residents to refuel only when essential to avoid panic buying and smuggling. Iran heavily subsidizes gasoline to prevent illicit trade across borders. This development comes amid escalated regional tensions following large-scale U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran starting

item.title: Minor Explosion at U.S. Embassy in Oslo Causes No Injuries, Norwegian Police Investigate
item.description: Early on Sunday, March 8, 2026, a loud explosion occurred at the U.S. Embassy compound in western Oslo, Norway, around 1:00 a.m. local time (0000 GMT). Norwegian police confirmed that the blast struck the entrance to the consular section, resulting in minor material damage but no injuries to personnel or bystanders. Eyewitnesses, including an 18-year-old high school student named Sebastian Toerstad who was driving nearby, reported hearing the blast and observing thick smoke rising from the area and covering the street. Police responded swiftly, deploying technicians in protective gear, explosive-detection dogs, drones, and a helicopter to search the vicinity for additional devices or suspects. No further explosive devices were discovered during the initial sweep. Authorities stated that the cause of the explosion and any individuals involved remain unknown at this stage, prompting an active investigation. The U.S. Embassy did not provide an immediate comment outside business hours, and

item.title: Investigation Underway Into Deadly Attack on Girls’ School in Minab, Iran
item.description: On March 5, 2026, a girls’ elementary school in Minab, Iran, was struck in a deadly attack that resulted in the deaths of at least 175 people, the majority of whom were children. Following the attack, online misinformation spread rapidly, with some claims falsely suggesting that the Iranian government itself was responsible. Journalist Nilo Tabrizy reported that despite Iran’s internet blackout, foreign reporters have verified the occurrence of the strike. Investigations are ongoing to determine whether the attack can be attributed to foreign powers, specifically the United States or Israel. The incident has sparked international concern and calls for accurate reporting amid a climate of disinformation. Democracy Now! has provided coverage of the situation, emphasizing independent verification and highlighting the difficulty of confirming details under current communications restrictions. The event underscores the devastating impact of military strikes on civilian populations and the c

item.title: Trump Administration's Higher Education Compact Largely Rejected by Universities, but Revisions May Be Underway
item.description: In October 2025, the Trump administration proposed the Compact for Academic Excellence in Higher Education to nine major U.S. universities, later expanding it to over 100 institutions. The deal promised advantages in federal funding, grants, research support, and other benefits in exchange for adopting significant policy changes. Key requirements included banning preferences based on race, ethnicity, sex, gender identity, political views, or other factors in admissions and hiring; mandating standardized tests like the SAT for applicants; promoting a 'vibrant marketplace of ideas' by addressing perceived biases against conservative viewpoints and potentially abolishing certain institutional units; requiring 'lawful force' against disruptive protesters; capping international student enrollment at around 15%; freezing tuition for American students; and reinforcing a binary view of sex as male or female. The proposal aligned with prior Supreme Court rulings against race-based admissions an

item.title: Federal-State Conflicts in U.S. Federalism: Immigration Enforcement and Historical Precedents
item.description: The article examines ongoing tensions between the federal government and U.S. states, particularly amid the Trump administration's aggressive immigration policies. Democratic-led states are refusing to cooperate with federal immigration arrests, while the federal government has withheld evidence in investigations into the deaths of two protesters, Renee Good and Alex Pretti, killed by federal officers during immigration enforcement protests in Minneapolis. The author, a constitutional law scholar specializing in federalism, explains that the U.S. Constitution establishes a system of divided powers as a check and balance. Federal law is supreme under the Supremacy Clause, especially in areas like immigration, which falls under federal authority, preventing states from nullifying it. However, the 10th Amendment reserves undelegated powers to the states, creating horizontal federalism where states can decline to enforce federal programs, as seen in marijuana legalization despite federal p

item.title: Study Shows Telehealth Plays a Key Role in Care for Medicare-Enrolled Older Adults
item.description: A recent study published in the Annals of Internal Medicine highlights that Americans aged 65 and older with Medicare coverage logged approximately 60 million telehealth visits per year from 2021 to 2023, with 31 million for mental health and 29 million for other medical conditions. Analysis of the nationally representative Medical Expenditures Panel Survey revealed that older adults using telehealth were generally in poorer health and faced more physical or functional limitations compared to those attending only in-person appointments. Telehealth usage surged during the COVID-19 pandemic, rising from 1.7% of Medicare patients in 2019 to 53% in 2021, as federal and private insurance policies expanded virtual care access. Permanent coverage now exists for mental health services, but coverage for other medical conditions is set to expire in 2027, leaving policymakers to decide on its continuation. The study emphasizes telehealth’s critical role in improving access to care, particularly f

item.title: NASA Observes Material Transfer Between Binary Asteroids
item.description: NASA's DART mission has provided the first direct visual evidence that asteroids in binary systems can exchange surface material. Using images captured before DART intentionally collided with Dimorphos, the moon of the asteroid Didymos, scientists observed faint fan-shaped streaks on Dimorphos' surface. These streaks indicate slow-moving debris, traveling at less than a walking pace, likely spun off from Didymos due to sunlight accelerating its rotation—a process known as the YORP effect. The debris created distinctive patterns on Dimorphos, demonstrating that near-Earth binary asteroids are more dynamic than previously believed. Researchers confirmed these findings by developing image-processing techniques to remove shadows and artifacts, running computer simulations, and conducting laboratory experiments with marbles and sand to replicate the fan-like streaks. The European Space Agency’s Hera mission, arriving at Didymos in December 2026, may provide further insight by observing whet

item.title: House Fire Contained in Laurel Canyon Amid Strong Santa Ana Winds
item.description: On the morning of March 7, 2026, a three-story home located at 8522 West Oak Court in Laurel Canyon, California, was heavily damaged by a fire ignited on a balcony. The blaze, fueled by intense Santa Ana winds, drew more than 100 firefighters and at least five engines to the narrow, steep streets of the hillside neighborhood. Despite the challenging terrain, the Los Angeles Fire Department contained the fire within 73 minutes, and no injuries were reported. The house, built in 1961 and measuring 960 square feet, has a history of celebrity ownership, including radio personality Elliot Mintz and Olympic gold medalist Ian Thorpe. Neighbors noted its minor celebrity status, with anecdotes linking John Lennon to the property in past decades. Firefighters deployed a structure defense team to monitor nearby homes and vegetation, ensuring the fire did not spread further. Authorities emphasized continued patrols through the night to manage any remaining hotspots. This incident serves as a remin

item.title: Discovery of an Isospin-Symmetric Island of Inversion in Molybdenum-84
item.description: Physicists have identified a surprising new 'Island of Inversion' in molybdenum-84, challenging long-held assumptions in nuclear physics. Traditionally, these regions, where atomic nuclei adopt highly distorted shapes instead of their usual orderly structure, were observed only in neutron-rich, unstable isotopes far from stability. However, molybdenum-84, with an equal number of protons and neutrons (Z = N = 42), demonstrates dramatic nuclear deformation unlike its close neighbor molybdenum-86. Using advanced rare isotope beams and sensitive gamma-ray detectors, researchers measured the lifetimes of excited nuclear states and found that Mo-84 undergoes large-scale collective particle-hole excitations. These excitations involve nucleons jumping to higher energy orbitals while leaving gaps in lower orbitals, resulting in a strongly deformed nucleus. In contrast, Mo-86 exhibits more modest excitations and remains nearly spherical. The study highlights the importance of three-nucleon inter

item.title: Exploring Malta in the Off-Season: History, Architecture, and Local Life
item.description: Traveling to Malta in January offers a unique experience, free from the usual tourist crowds and high prices of the peak season. The author describes arriving at night during rainy conditions and renting a car with left-side driving, which took some adjustment. Malta’s cars are largely imported, including many Japanese models, due to the island’s driving conventions. The country features a striking sandy-colored architecture made from Globigerina limestone, which contains fossilized marine microorganisms. Notable architectural highlights include covered balconies called gallariji and the abundance of large churches and basilicas, such as the Rotunda of Mosta and the parish church of Xewkija. Malta’s history is deeply tied to the Knights Hospitaller, who defended the island from the Ottoman Empire during the Great Siege of 1565 and shaped the capital, Valletta. The article also touches on the Maltese language, a unique blend of Arabic, Italian, and English influences. Overall, the piece

item.title: Trump Faces Diplomatic Strain as UK Maintains Distance Over Iran Conflict
item.description: During a visit to Dover Air Base for the dignified transfer of six fallen U.S. soldiers, President Donald Trump appeared visibly affected, yet his attention quickly shifted to diplomatic concerns with the United Kingdom. The article highlights tensions in the so-called 'special relationship,' as UK Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer has resisted backing Trump's position on the conflict in Iran. This comes amid widespread domestic opposition to the military intervention, with rising fears of higher gas prices and skepticism over the campaign's legality. The report also contrasts the solemnity of the Dover ceremony with White House social media posts portraying the Iran strikes as stylized, gamified videos, raising questions about tone and sensitivity. The coverage emphasizes the gravity and human cost of the military action, including the reported death of over 160 children in a bombing of a school, attributed by Trump to Iran. The article portrays Trump’s struggle to secure political capi

item.title: Legal Penalties in Gulf States for Sharing War Footage on Social Media
item.description: Authorities in several Gulf countries, including the UAE, Bahrain, Kuwait, and Qatar, have issued strict warnings regarding the sharing of digital content related to the ongoing conflict in the region. The UAE prosecutor has stated that anyone sharing or forwarding content from unknown sources will be held legally responsible, even if they are not the original creator. The Dubai police have highlighted that spreading rumors can result in fines of at least 200,000 AED (approximately $54,458) and up to two years in prison for both residents and tourists. Bahrain recently detained two individuals for recording and sharing videos of Iranian attacks, citing security risks and the potential to mislead public opinion. Similarly, Kuwait and Qatar have warned residents against filming aerial threats, emphasizing that such actions may induce public anxiety and disseminate misinformation. These measures reflect a broader regional effort to control information about the war and maintain public ord

item.title: European Nations Debate Responses to Surging Energy Costs Amid Global Tensions
item.description: European countries are confronting sharp disagreements over how to address skyrocketing energy prices triggered by ongoing conflicts in the Middle East and the lingering effects of the Ukraine war. Gas prices in Europe have surged to levels not seen since 2023, sparking heated debates over energy regulation. Central to the discussion is the EU's 'merit-order' electricity pricing system, which often makes renewable energy appear more expensive than it should be. Heavy industries are pushing for reforms, claiming that the current system no longer fits a crisis heavily influenced by fossil fuels, while power producers insist that the existing framework guarantees fair pricing and investment incentives. Some governments, particularly Germany and Italy, are exploring temporary relaxations of carbon regulations to reduce energy costs, although such measures face resistance from environmental advocates and EU green policy proponents. Efforts to increase renewable energy usage face long timeli

item.title: Former Israeli Prime Minister Warns of Strategic Threat from Turkey
item.description: Former Israeli Prime Minister Naftali Bennett recently issued strong statements regarding Turkey and its president, Recep Tayyip Erdogan, labeling Ankara as a new strategic threat to Israel's security. Bennett accused Turkey of supporting not only Iran but also various groups in the Middle East that Israel considers terrorist organizations. In an interview, he described Erdogan as a sophisticated and dangerous adversary aiming to encircle Israel. Bennett urged Israel and its allies to take Ankara’s actions seriously and to implement a comprehensive containment strategy, emphasizing that measures should not be limited to Iran alone but should include systemic actions against Turkey. He also highlighted the influence of certain political and financial networks, pointing to Qatar and Turkey’s activities in Syria and Gaza, which he claims strengthen connections tied to the Muslim Brotherhood. Bennett suggested that Israel might consider formally recognizing Turkey as a hostile state. His c

item.title: Dubai Faces Shortages in Fresh Food Supplies Amid Regional Tensions
item.description: Dubai, a major hub in the Gulf region, is facing a temporary shortage of fresh food supplies due to disruptions in global logistics and heightened geopolitical tensions. Stefan Paul, director of Kuehne & Nagel, one of the world's largest logistics companies, reported that the city has fresh food reserves for only about ten days. While this does not indicate an immediate risk of famine, it highlights the vulnerability of cities that rely heavily on imported fresh produce. Approximately 70% of food shipments to Gulf countries pass through the Strait of Hormuz, and recent restrictions have reduced air cargo capacity to the Middle East by 22% between February 28 and March 3. The Jebel Ali port, responsible for serving 50 million people and most of Dubai's perishable imports, briefly suspended operations, affecting the supply of fruits, vegetables, dairy, and herbs. Although Dubai has substantial reserves of dry goods, frozen products, and strategic grains, the city is currently facing the

item.title: The Growing Vulnerability of Data Centers in Military Conflicts
item.description: Recent attacks in Iran have highlighted the strategic vulnerability of data centers in modern warfare. As critical infrastructure for both economic and governmental operations, these facilities are increasingly targeted during conflicts. In the Gulf region, U.S. and Israeli attacks prompted retaliatory strikes by Iran, leading to the destruction of three Amazon Web Services (AWS) data centers—two in the United Arab Emirates and one in Bahrain—causing temporary disruptions in digital services. Although these attacks did not immediately halt operations, AWS advised clients to relocate workloads to other countries to maintain service continuity. Iran has long anticipated such risks, relocating portions of its digital infrastructure underground to create fortified data centers, including the National Iranian Network (NIN), which allows the government to maintain essential services during external attacks. Globally, other countries have adopted similar measures. In the United States, a data

item.title: Sen. Katie Britt Discusses Trump, Foreign Conflicts, and Gridlock in Washington
item.description: This video segment features Senator Katie Britt discussing how President Donald Trump’s agenda intersects with ongoing international conflicts and the persistent gridlock in Washington, D.C. She reflects on how partisan divisions in Congress have complicated efforts to respond decisively to foreign crises and to advance domestic priorities. Britt emphasizes that repeated obstruction and brinkmanship have slowed confirmations, stalled legislation, and undermined public confidence in government institutions, even when there is nominal agreement on key goals such as national security and economic stability. She also underscores Trump’s argument that entrenched Washington practices and partisan tactics have made it harder for his administration to implement its policies efficiently. In the conversation, Britt ties current wars and security challenges abroad to the need for a more functional Congress that can act quickly on defense, foreign aid, and diplomatic initiatives. She points to her

item.title: Trump States No Interest in Negotiating with Iran, Suggests War May End Only After Destruction of Its Military and Leadership
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump, speaking aboard Air Force One, declared he has no interest in negotiating with Iran amid the ongoing U.S.-Israeli air campaign now in its second week. He suggested the conflict might conclude only when Iran's military is destroyed and no leadership remains capable of surrendering, stating, 'At some point, I don’t think there will be anybody left maybe to say ‘We surrender.’' The war, which escalated with joint strikes targeting Iranian missile sites, command centers, and leadership, has spread regionally. Iran has retaliated by launching attacks on U.S. facilities in Gulf states like the UAE, Bahrain, and others, as well as on Israel. Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian apologized to neighboring countries for strikes affecting them, urging them not to join attacks on Iran, while suspending further assaults on nearby states unless provoked from their territory—a move that drew domestic criticism from hardliners and the Revolutionary Guards. Despite claims of

item.title: Bronze Age Sheep Bone Yields First Ancient Yersinia pestis Genome in Livestock, Shedding Light on Prehistoric Plague Spread
item.description: Researchers have identified the bacterium Yersinia pestis in the remains of a domesticated sheep dating back about 4,000 years from the Arkaim site in the Southern Ural Mountains, Russia. This marks the first detection of the pathogen in a non-human host from the Bronze Age, belonging to the Late Neolithic-Bronze Age (LNBA) lineage that infected humans across Eurasia for nearly 2,000 years starting around 5,000 years ago. Unlike the later Black Death strain, this ancient version lacked flea-transmission capabilities, leaving scientists puzzled about its wide geographic spread. The discovery, published in Cell, suggests livestock like sheep played a key role in transmission, possibly alongside an unidentified natural reservoir such as steppe rodents or migratory birds. The sheep's infection closely matches strains found in nearby human remains, indicating shared exposure dynamics tied to pastoralist lifestyles of the Sintashta culture, known for horse riding, bronze tools, and large her

item.title: Federal Funding Uncertainty Disrupts State Public Health Programs Despite Later Restorations
item.description: Since early 2025, the federal government has suspended several large public health grants to states, only to restore them after legal challenges and court interventions. A notable example occurred on February 13, 2026, when approximately $600 million in grants to four states were briefly halted before a federal court blocked the action. While these may appear as typical intergovernmental disputes from the outside, they generate significant internal uncertainty for state and local public health agencies. This instability interrupts long-term prevention programs, including vaccination clinics, disease surveillance, opioid response efforts, maternal health initiatives, and restaurant inspections. Public health relies on multiyear planning, stable staffing, and consistent partnerships funded largely by federal grants. When funding is paused—even temporarily—agencies slow hiring, draft contingency plans, face staff turnover (with surveys showing high numbers of public health workers conside

item.title: Machine Learning Identifies Key Indicator of Fast Ion Movement in Solid-State Batteries
item.description: Researchers have developed a machine learning approach that could significantly advance the development of all-solid-state batteries (ASSBs), which are safer and potentially more energy-dense than conventional lithium-ion batteries. A major challenge in these batteries is identifying materials that allow ions to move rapidly through solid electrolytes. Traditional experimental and computational methods are often slow or computationally expensive, particularly for systems where ions move in a liquid-like manner. The new workflow combines machine learning force fields with tensorial models to simulate Raman spectra, revealing a distinctive low-frequency signal that corresponds to rapid ion motion disrupting crystal symmetry. This signal provides a clear indicator of high ionic mobility. The approach was validated using sodium-ion conducting materials like Na3SbS4, confirming that strong low-frequency Raman features correlate with fast ionic conduction, while materials with hopping-based

item.title: Gulf States Deploy Layered Air-Defense Systems Against Iranian Missiles and Drones
item.description: In the past week, Gulf countries have faced multiple missile and drone attacks from Iran, prompted by US-Israeli strikes that killed Iranian supreme leader Ali Khamenei. Governments in the UAE, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Kuwait, Bahrain, Oman, and Jordan have activated their air-defense networks to intercept incoming threats. The UAE relies on a layered system including THAAD and Patriot missiles, detecting 196 ballistic missiles and successfully intercepting 181, though some missiles still caused casualties and infrastructure damage. Saudi Arabia, with an extensive air-defense network, intercepted multiple drones and cruise missiles targeting cities and critical energy infrastructure like the Ras Tanura oil refinery. Qatar contributes to regional early-warning networks via Al Udeid Air Base, intercepting most of the missiles, drones, and cruise missiles launched toward it. Kuwait and Bahrain also relied on Patriot systems, though debris from interceptions caused some civilian damage. Oman,

item.title: Two Arrested After Suspicious Devices Thrown During Protests Outside NYC Mayor’s Residence
item.description: During protests outside Gracie Mansion, the official residence of New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani, tensions between opposing groups escalated, resulting in the deployment of ignited suspicious devices. The incident occurred amid an anti-Islam protest led by right-wing influencer and pardoned January 6 rioter Jake Lang, attended by roughly 20 people, and a counter-protest titled 'Drive the Nazis Out of New York,' which drew approximately 125 participants. According to NYPD Commissioner Jessica S. Tisch, one counter-protester, 18, lit and threw a device toward the protest area, and later retrieved and ignited a second device with the assistance of a 19-year-old. Both men were arrested along with four others for related offenses, including the use of pepper spray and disorderly conduct. The devices, described as jars wrapped in black tape containing bolts, screws, and a hobby fuse, were analyzed by the bomb squad and sent for further testing. No injuries were reported, and the mayor an

item.title: Addressing the Needs of Single Employees in Family-Focused Workplaces
item.description: Modern workplaces have increasingly adapted to support dual-career couples and working parents by providing flexible hours, paid parental leave, and childcare subsidies. While these measures are beneficial, they often overlook a significant portion of the workforce: single employees without dependents. Currently, nearly half of U.S. adults are unmarried, and single-person households are the most common living arrangement. Yet, many HR policies still assume a traditional family structure, favoring married employees or those raising children. This can manifest in scheduling expectations, benefits allocation, and leave policies that disadvantage singles, who may be expected to work holidays, travel more, or take less desirable vacation times. Moreover, benefits like health coverage, retirement contributions, and bereavement leave often provide more value to married employees, while singles’ broader support networks, including chosen family, are rarely recognized. Experts suggest that comp

item.title: Alphabet Approves Performance-Based Compensation Package for CEO Sundar Pichai Worth Up to $692 Million Over Three Years
item.description: Alphabet, Google's parent company, has approved a new three-year compensation package for CEO Sundar Pichai that could total up to $692 million, according to an SEC filing reported on March 7, 2026. The package keeps his base salary unchanged at $2 million per year (totaling $6 million over three years) and eliminates any annual bonus. The vast majority of the potential payout comes from equity awards, including restricted stock units valued at $84 million that vest gradually over the period as long as he remains with the company. Additional performance stock units are tied to Alphabet's total shareholder return compared to S&P 100 peers. Notably, this deal introduces new incentives linked to the performance and valuation growth of Alphabet's 'Other Bets' subsidiaries, specifically Waymo (autonomous vehicles) and Wing (drone delivery), marking a first for tying CEO pay directly to these ventures' success. This structure could position Pichai as one of the highest-paid executives global

item.title: Obama Delivers Eulogy at Rev. Jesse Jackson's Final Chicago Memorial
item.description: Former President Barack Obama delivered a moving eulogy at the final public tribute to civil rights icon Rev. Jesse Jackson held at a Chicago church on March 6, 2026. Jackson, who passed away at age 84 last month after battling a rare neurological disorder, was honored by Obama, former Presidents Joe Biden and Bill Clinton, and former Vice President Kamala Harris. Obama credited Jackson's groundbreaking 1980s presidential campaigns with paving the way for Black leaders, including his own path to the presidency, stating, 'He paved the road for so many others to follow' and emphasizing Jackson's courage and audacity. He reflected on Jackson's message of empowerment to the overlooked and dispossessed, saying Jackson stepped forward repeatedly when the poor needed a champion. Obama noted the challenging times, remarking, 'We are living in a time when it can be hard to hope,' amid assaults on democratic institutions, yet urged carrying forward Jackson's legacy of hope and service. Attendees

item.title: Economist Proposes Measuring Poverty as a Continuous Spectrum to Better Capture Inequality's Impact
item.description: An economist critiques the traditional use of fixed poverty lines in the United States, which have remained largely unchanged since the 1960s. The piece references Wall Street investor Michael W. Green's late-2025 argument to raise the poverty threshold for a family of four from about $33,000 to $140,000 annually, which generated widespread media discussion. However, the author argues that debates over specific thresholds—whether $33,000, $100,000, or $140,000—miss the point, as no single arbitrary line truly separates poverty from adequacy. Instead, poverty should be viewed as a spectrum, where lower income always means greater hardship without a magical cutoff. The author introduces 'average poverty,' a measure defined as the inverse of average income (expressed in days, hours, or minutes per dollar earned, adjusted for purchasing power). This approach reveals that the U.S. requires an average of 63 minutes to earn $1, far higher than in countries like the UK (34 minutes), France (un

item.title: Britney Spears arrested in California on suspicion of driving under the influence
item.description: Britney Spears, the 44-year-old pop singer, was arrested in California on Wednesday evening for allegedly driving under the influence of a combination of drugs and alcohol. Police reported that Spears was driving a black BMW 430i erratically at high speed on southbound US-101 near Newbury Park. Officers observed signs of impairment and conducted a series of field sobriety tests before taking her into custody. Spears spent the night in Ventura County Main Jail and was released around 6 a.m. local time. She is scheduled to appear in court on May 4. Following the incident, Spears’ Instagram account, which had more than 42 million followers, was deactivated. Her representative described the incident as 'completely inexcusable' and emphasized that Spears plans to comply with the law and take steps toward positive change, including receiving support from loved ones. This is not Spears’ first legal issue related to driving; in 2007, she avoided a criminal hit-and-run trial by paying for damag

item.title: Kristi Noem Removed from Position, Markwayne Mullin Appointed
item.description: In a recent development in U.S. political circles, Kristi Noem, previously nicknamed 'ICE Queen' and 'ICE Barbie,' has been removed from her position, reportedly by order of former President Donald Trump. Noem's departure paves the way for Markwayne Mullin to assume the role she vacated. This change is part of a broader effort by Trump to reorganize and consolidate influence within his circle. Meanwhile, Trump has expressed his desire to play a role in selecting the next Iranian Supreme Leader, highlighting ongoing international interests and tensions. Mark Stone, reporting from Corpus Christi, Texas, is gathering opinions from Americans and Trump supporters regarding the war in Iran. Additionally, Argentine soccer star Lionel Messi visited the White House, meeting with the president in a separate event that drew public attention. The news has been covered as part of the Trump100 podcast series, which provides updates on political maneuvers, international matters, and high-profile pers

item.title: OpenAI's Head of Robotics Resigns Over Concerns Regarding Pentagon AI Agreement
item.description:
Caitlin Kalinowski, who led OpenAI's robotics team after joining from Meta in November 2024, announced her resignation on March 7, 2026, citing ethical concerns related to the company's recent agreement with the U.S. Department of Defense. In social media posts, she expressed that while AI plays a vital role in national security, issues such as warrantless surveillance of Americans and fully autonomous lethal weapons required more thorough discussion and safeguards than were provided. She described her departure as a matter of principle rather than personal conflict, maintaining respect for CEO Sam Altman and the team, and later clarified her primary issue was the rushed nature of the announcement without properly defined guardrails, framing it as a governance problem.
The Pentagon deal, announced shortly after failed negotiations with rival Anthropic (which sought strict limits on domestic surveillance and autonomous weapons, leading to its designation as a supply-chain risk), allows

item.title: Democrats Assess Opportunity in Texas’ 23rd District After Gonzales Withdraws
item.description: Texas’ 23rd Congressional District may become unexpectedly competitive following Rep. Tony Gonzales’ decision to withdraw from his reelection campaign amid a personal scandal. Gonzales’ exit has left Brandon Herrera, a social media personality and gun rights advocate known as “TheAKGuy,” as the Republican nominee. Herrera’s controversial online presence, which includes past Nazi imagery and membership in the Sons of Confederate Veterans, has drawn criticism and could be a vulnerability in the general election. Democrats see an opening in a district historically held by Republicans but recently showing signs of changing demographics, including strong Latino voter turnout in recent primaries. Katy Padilla Stout, the Democratic nominee, could leverage these factors to compete for a seat that Trump won by 17 points in 2024 but that Clinton narrowly carried in 2016. While national Democratic organizations have not yet committed resources to the district, local party leaders are optimistic t

item.title: Trump Praises and Imitates Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum's Voice During Summit Remarks on Cartels
item.description: At the Shield of the Americas summit held on March 7, 2026, at his Trump National Doral golf club in Florida, President Donald Trump addressed a group of Latin American leaders on issues including drug cartel violence originating from Mexico. During his remarks, Trump expressed admiration for Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum, describing her as a 'very good person,' a 'beautiful woman,' and someone with a 'beautiful voice.'
